name: International
description: International standard-setters and organisations across tax, finance, trade, labour, IP, accounting and private international law: OECD, IMF, World Bank, WTO, ILO, BIS, IOSCO, FATF, WIPO, the IFRS Foundation, the Hague Conference and the UN
instructions: Prioritise the OECD Model Tax Convention, Transfer Pricing Guidelines and BEPS action reports for international tax; for Pillar One/Two rely on OECD/G20 Inclusive Framework publications and state their release date — the guidance evolves quickly

International soft law is versioned: always cite full document title, publishing body, edition/year and paragraph number; distinguish binding treaty law from model instruments, guidelines and recommendations

Route by body: banking prudential standards → Basel framework document number (e.g. BCBS 189) from bis.org; securities → IOSCO principles/reports; AML/CFT → FATF Recommendations by number plus the country mutual-evaluation report; labour standards → ILO Conventions by number (ilo.org); accounting → IFRS Standards from the IFRS Foundation (ifrs.org)
For trade, cite WTO agreements and panel/Appellate Body reports (wto.org) by dispute number (DS...); for private international law cite the Hague Conventions from HCCH (hcch.net) by title and year

For macro-financial and country data and legal frameworks, use the IMF (imf.org, incl. Article IV and technical papers) and World Bank (worldbank.org, spread across documents/openknowledge/data subdomains)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) treaty, (ii) model instrument, (iii) organisation guideline/standard, (iv) report or working paper — and give the pinpoint (article, paragraph, principle number) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: Official OECD documents (C(...) decisions, declassified papers) live on one.oecd.org, covered by oecd.org scoping and indexed; the OECD iLibrary hosts the Guidelines and Model Convention
WIPO treaty texts and national IP laws are under WIPO Lex — searching 'WIPO Lex' plus the treaty or country name lands on the authoritative text at wipo.int/wipolex

World Bank material is spread across documents.worldbank.org, openknowledge.worldbank.org and data.worldbank.org (all covered by worldbank.org); IMF Article IV reports and the eLibrary are on imf.org (which blocks bare curl but loads via browser)
IFRS Standards require the ifrs.org account for full text of some materials; Basel/BCBS and FATF documents index by their own numbering

name: European Union
description: European Union law and institutions: EUR-Lex, the Court of Justice, the European Council and Council, Parliament, Commission and the Publications Office, the ECB and the EU financial and sectoral agencies (EDPB/EDPS, ESMA, EBA, EIOPA, EUIPO, EMA), plus the ECtHR (Council of Europe)
instructions: Cite EU legislation from EUR-Lex by act type, number and year and by CELEX identifier (Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — CELEX 32016R0679), stating the consolidated-version date; distinguish regulations (directly applicable), directives (requiring national transposition) and decisions

Cite CJEU case law by ECLI and case number from curia.europa.eu (Case C-311/18, ECLI:EU:C:2020:559), distinguishing the Court of Justice from the General Court (T- cases); cite Advocate General opinions separately
The ECtHR (echr.coe.int) is a Council of Europe body, not an EU institution — cite its judgments by application number and HUDOC reference and keep the ECHR system distinct from EU law

Route by institution: legislative procedure and co-legislators → Parliament (europarl.europa.eu) and Council (consilium.europa.eu); Commission guidance, infringement and soft law → ec.europa.eu; official publication → the Publications Office (op.europa.eu)

Route by agency: monetary policy and banking supervision (SSM) → ECB (ecb.europa.eu); data protection → EDPB (edpb.europa.eu, guidelines) and the EU's own supervisor EDPS (edps.europa.eu); securities → ESMA; banking → EBA; insurance/pensions → EIOPA; EU trade marks and designs → EUIPO (euipo.europa.eu); medicines → EMA (ema.europa.eu)
Distinguish binding EU law from soft law: EBA/ESMA/EIOPA guidelines operate on a 'comply-or-explain' basis and EDPB guidelines interpret but do not enact — label each and cite by its own reference

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Treaty/Regulation/Directive (EUR-Lex CELEX), (ii) CJEU/ECtHR judgment (ECLI/application no.), (iii) Commission/agency soft law, (iv) national transposition where a directive is in play — and give the pinpoint (article, paragraph) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: EUR-Lex is the authoritative source with stable CELEX numbers and consolidated versions; search the CELEX or the act's natural name; the Official Journal is published via op.europa.eu
curia.europa.eu uses the case-number search at curia.europa.eu/juris — the site's own search is weak, so query by case number or ECLI; HUDOC (hudoc.echr.coe.int) serves ECtHR judgments
Several institution/agency sites (consilium.europa.eu, edps.europa.eu, euipo.europa.eu) block bare curl but load via browser
The ESAs (ESMA/EBA/EIOPA) publish their guidelines, Q&As and the Single Rulebook on their own domains; EUR-Lex remains authoritative for the underlying Level 1/2 measures

name: Spain
description: Spain, all legal disciplines: legislation (BOE), constitutional and all-order case law (TC, CENDOJ), Parliament (Congreso, Senado) and the state authorities across civil, criminal, commercial/registry, labour and social security, administrative, tax, financial, competition, data protection, consumer, IP, environment, health and immigration law
instructions: For any discipline the primary norm is the consolidated text in the BOE: cite legislation by official name and BOE identifier (e.g. Ley 27/2014 — BOE-A-2014-12328), prefer the consolidated version (boe.es/buscar/act.php) and state its last-update date; ministry and regulator pages are secondary guidance

Cite judgments by ECLI across every jurisdictional order — civil, penal, contencioso-administrativo and social — from CENDOJ (e.g. ECLI:ES:TS:2023:1234); cite Tribunal Constitucional as STC/ATC number/year (also published in the BOE with a BOE-A identifier)

Route each question to its authority: labour and social security → mites.gob.es, seg-social.es, sepe.es and the social order of CENDOJ; criminal → fiscal.es and the penal order; commercial and registry → rmc.es, registradores.es and DGSJFP resolutions; consumer → dsca.gob.es; data protection → aepd.es; competition and regulated sectors (energy, telecoms, transport) → cnmc.es; securities → cnmv.es; banking → bde.es; insurance and pensions → dgsfp.mineco.gob.es; industrial and intellectual property → oepm.es; environment and energy → miteco.gob.es; health and medicines → sanidad.gob.es, aemps.gob.es; immigration → inclusion.gob.es and interior.gob.es

For registry and company law cite DGSJFP (formerly DGRN) resolutions by date and their BOE publication; for public-law advisory questions cite Consejo de Estado dictámenes by number/year (consejo-estado.es)

Distinguish binding law from interpretive criteria: DGT consultas (tax), Fiscalía circulares/consultas/instrucciones (criminal), AEPD guías and regulator guidelines are administrative or interpretive positions, not statute — label them as such and cite by type, number and year

Tax specifics: cite DGT consultas by number and date (e.g. DGT V0213-21, de 9-2-2021) and state whether vinculante ('V' prefix, art. 89 LGT); the tax hierarchy is ley and reglamento (BOE consolidated) → TEAC/TEAR economic-administrative doctrine and Tribunal Supremo case law → DGT consultas vinculantes → AEAT guidance

This source is state-level: autonomic (CCAA) and local law is not in the BOE — when a question turns on autonomic or municipal competence, flag it and note the relevant regional gazette (the Boletín/Diari Oficial of the comunidad) is outside this source's scope

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) statute/reglamento, (ii) case law, (iii) administrative doctrine, (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (article, fundamento or apartado) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: BOE is fully server-rendered — fetch directly with WebFetch, no browser: consolidated legislation at boe.es/buscar/act.php?id=BOE-A-YYYY-NNNNN and individual gazette items (including Tribunal Constitucional judgments, which are published in the BOE) at boe.es/diario_boe/txt.php?id=BOE-A-YYYY-NNNNN

Tribunal Constitucional case law is searchable at hj.tribunalconstitucional.es (a subdomain of tribunalconstitucional.es, covered by its scoping); individual STC/ATC also resolve as BOE-A items on boe.es and are fully fetchable there

DGT consultas: the petete.tributos.hacienda.gob.es/consultas/ interface (including ?num_consulta=V1601-22) is a jQuery/AJAX app — a plain fetch returns only an empty shell ('Cargando formulario...'). The doctrine loads via AJAX POST to /consultas/do/search and /consultas/do/document, whose query form uses opaque NMCMP/OPCMP/VLCMP field tuples not worth reconstructing by hand; this is one of the few sources here where browser automation against the petete search box is strictly necessary
TEAC/TEAR economic-administrative doctrine is in the DYCTEA database at serviciostelematicosext.hacienda.gob.es/TEAC/DYCTEA/ (a subdomain of hacienda.gob.es, so covered by its scoping)

CENDOJ case-law search (poderjudicial.es/search/indexAN.jsp) is a form page whose result list is JS/POST-driven — a plain fetch returns only the empty form, so use browser automation to run the query and open the judgment. Judgments already identified by ECLI can often be read as server-rendered HTML elsewhere first (e.g. Tribunal Constitucional rulings via their BOE-A identifier on boe.es) — try that with WebFetch before opening the browser

Company law: BORME is published under boe.es/diario_borme/ and the Registro Mercantil Central is at rmc.es; registradores.es blocks plain fetching (WAF) — reach it with browser automation when a registry resolution is only there

interior.gob.es and consejo-estado.es return 403 to a plain fetch of their home page — fetch the specific document URL directly, or fall back to browser automation, rather than crawling from the landing page
The former AEAT domain seat.agenciatributaria.gob.es no longer resolves — the sede is sede.agenciatributaria.gob.es, well indexed including English pages under /Sede/en_gb/
Banco de España's legislation database is at app.bde.es/clf_www/leyes.jsp and CNMC decisions at cnmc.es/expedientes/ (both covered by their parent-domain scoping)

name: France
description: France, all legal disciplines: consolidated codes and the JORF gazette (Légifrance), the Constitutional Council, both supreme courts (Conseil d'État for administrative and tax litigation, Cour de cassation for civil and criminal), both chambers of parliament (Assemblée nationale, Sénat) and the state authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, labour and social, administrative, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP, consumer, environment and energy, health and immigration law
instructions: For any discipline the primary norm is the consolidated code or statute on Légifrance: cite codes by article and code name (article 1741 du CGI, article 1240 du Code civil, article L1232-1 du Code du travail) stating the version-in-force date, and cite enactments by their JORF publication (loi/décret n° YYYY-NNN, JORF of the date). Ministry and regulator pages are secondary guidance, not statute

Route each question to its supreme court: administrative and tax litigation → Conseil d'État (conseil-etat.fr, cited CE, 6 mars 2025, n° 491833); civil and criminal → Cour de cassation (courdecassation.fr, cited by chamber, date and pourvoi n°, e.g. Cass. soc., 12 mars 2025, n° 23-14.567 — the chambre sociale handles labour); constitutionality → Conseil constitutionnel (conseil-constitutionnel.fr, DC and QPC decisions cited n° YYYY-NNN DC / QPC)

For tax, the binding administrative doctrine is BOFiP (bofip.impots.gouv.fr) — cite the document identifier with its date (BOI-CTX-DG-20-10-10-20140625); doctrine published there is opposable to the administration (rescrit, LPF art. L80 A). impots.gouv.fr carries practical DGFiP guidance, not opposable doctrine

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: financial markets and asset management → AMF (amf-france.org, doctrine cited by DOC number, e.g. DOC-2012-19 — positions bind, recommendations do not); banking and insurance supervision → ACPR at acpr.banque-france.fr and monetary matters → Banque de France (banque-france.fr); competition and merger control → Autorité de la concurrence (autoritedelaconcurrence.fr); data protection → CNIL (cnil.fr, délibérations and sanctions); industrial property → INPI (inpi.fr); energy → CRE (cre.fr); medicines and devices → ANSM (ansm.sante.fr) and health-technology assessment → HAS (has-sante.fr); asylum → OFPRA (ofpra.fr)

For labour and social matters distinguish ministry policy and circulaires (travail-emploi.gouv.fr) from the adjudicating courts (conseil de prud'hommes at first instance, chambre sociale of the Cour de cassation at the top); for consumer and economic regulation cite the DGCCRF, which sits under economie.gouv.fr; for environment cite the code de l'environnement on Légifrance and ministry guidance at ecologie.gouv.fr

The Défenseur des droits (defenseurdesdroits.fr) issues décisions and avis on discrimination, public-service relations, children's rights and ethics of security — interpretive, not binding; service-public.fr is authoritative plain-language administrative information (fiches pratiques), useful for procedure but not a primary legal source to cite for the rule itself

Distinguish binding law from interpretive positions throughout: BOFiP and AMF positions are opposable, but circulaires, CNIL guidelines, HAS recommendations and Défenseur des droits décisions are interpretive or soft law — label each by type, number and date

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) code/loi/décret, (ii) court decision, (iii) opposable administrative doctrine (BOFiP, rescrit, AMF position), (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (article, considérant or § of the doctrine) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: Légifrance URLs carry stable identifiers (LEGITEXT for codes, LEGIARTI for articles, JORFTEXT for gazette texts, JURITEXT/CETATEXT for case law) and are well indexed — searching the code + article number lands directly on the consolidated article; the JORF is also on Légifrance (jorf/id/JORFTEXT...)
BOFiP identifiers (BOI-...-YYYYMMDD) resolve at bofip.impots.gouv.fr and are indexed — search the BOI reference directly

Conseil d'État decisions are under conseil-etat.fr/fr/arianeweb/CE/decision/<date>/<number> (ArianeWeb); Cour de cassation decisions live at courdecassation.fr/decision/<id> and in the open Judilibre API/portal — the site search is JS-driven, so for a known pourvoi number open the decision URL directly or use browser automation
QPC commentary and case tracking are on qpc360.conseil-constitutionnel.fr (covered by conseil-constitutionnel.fr scoping)
AMF doctrine is filed under amf-france.org/fr/reglementation by DOC number; ACPR material is at acpr.banque-france.fr (covered by banque-france.fr scoping)

INPI register searches (data.inpi.fr for trade marks/companies, the RNE) are separate query applications — reach the data.inpi.fr interface directly and use browser automation if the form is JS-driven

Several of these sites sit behind a WAF that returns 403 (or no response) to a bare fetch of the home page — legifrance.gouv.fr, banque-france.fr, acpr.banque-france.fr and economie.gouv.fr among them — while the pages load normally in a browser and via WebFetch: fetch the specific document/article URL directly, or fall back to browser automation, rather than crawling from the landing page

name: Germany
description: Germany, all legal disciplines: consolidated federal law (Gesetze im Internet) and the electronic Bundesgesetzblatt (recht.bund.de), the federal case-law database (rechtsprechung-im-internet.de), all five supreme federal courts and the Constitutional Court (BVerfG, BGH, BFH, BVerwG, BAG, BSG), both chambers of parliament (Bundestag, Bundesrat) and the federal authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, labour and social, administrative, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP, consumer/network, environment, health and immigration law
instructions: For any discipline the primary norm is the consolidated federal text on gesetze-im-internet.de: cite statutes by section and short title (§ 32a EStG, § 823 BGB, § 1 KSchG) and state the version-in-force; cite enactments and amendments by Bundesgesetzblatt reference (BGBl. year, part, number) — since 2023 the official BGBl is electronic at recht.bund.de. Ministry and regulator pages are secondary guidance, not statute

Route each question to its court by jurisdictional order: constitutional → BVerfG (bundesverfassungsgericht.de); civil and criminal → BGH (bundesgerichtshof.de); tax → BFH (bundesfinanzhof.de); general administrative → BVerwG (bundesverwaltungsgericht.de); labour → BAG (bundesarbeitsgericht.de); social security → BSG (bundessozialgericht.de). Free full text of all federal decisions is consolidated at rechtsprechung-im-internet.de

Cite court decisions by court, date and docket number (BFH, Urteil vom 11.7.2023, X R 17/22; BVerfG 2 BvR 1440/23; BGH I ZR 88/23; BAG 2 AZR 483/22), adding the ECLI where available (ECLI:DE:BGH:2024:...); a BFH decision binds the administration generally only once published in the Bundessteuerblatt II

Route regulatory questions to the competent federal authority: financial markets, banking and insurance supervision → BaFin (bafin.de); monetary and central-banking matters → Bundesbank (bundesbank.de); competition and merger control → Bundeskartellamt (bundeskartellamt.de); data protection → BfDI (bfdi.bund.de) at federal level; industrial property (patents, trade marks, designs) → DPMA (dpma.de); energy, telecoms, post and rail plus network/consumer matters → Bundesnetzagentur (bundesnetzagentur.de); medicines and medical devices → BfArM (bfarm.de); asylum and migration → BAMF (bamf.de)

For tax, BMF-Schreiben (bundesfinanzministerium.de) are the administrative guidance (Verwaltungsanweisungen) binding on the tax administration but not on the courts — cite by date and file reference; the Bundeszentralamt für Steuern (bzst.de) handles withholding-tax relief, VAT-ID and international tax administration

For labour and social matters distinguish the ministry policy (BMAS, bmas.de) from the adjudicating courts (BAG for labour, BSG for social security); for environment cite the ministry (BMUV, bmuv.de) and the Umweltbundesamt (umweltbundesamt.de) as the technical authority

Germany is a federal state: this source is the federal (Bund) level. When a question turns on Land competence (police law, education, much of building and environmental enforcement, court practice of the Oberlandesgerichte/Landgerichte), flag it and note the relevant Land gazette and courts are outside this source's scope

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Gesetz/Verordnung, (ii) court decision, (iii) administrative guidance (BMF-/BaFin-Schreiben, Verwaltungsanweisung), (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (§, Randnummer or Leitsatz) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: gesetze-im-internet.de exposes every section at a stable URL (e.g. /estg/__32a.html) and is fully server-rendered — fetch directly with WebFetch; search the statute abbreviation plus § number. A plain curl HEAD may return no status (UA/TLS filtering) even though WebFetch and browsers load it fine
Official Bundesgesetzblatt PDFs are at recht.bund.de/bgbl/... and are indexed; use recht.bund.de for promulgated texts and gesetze-im-internet.de for consolidated ones

rechtsprechung-im-internet.de is a jPortal application (jportal/portal/... with jsessionid): the result list is session/POST-driven, so a plain fetch of the search page returns only the portal shell — use browser automation to run the query, or open a decision already identified by court+docket directly from the deciding court's own site
BFH decisions have English-language detail pages under bundesfinanzhof.de/en/entscheidungen/...; BGH decisions are served from juris.bundesgerichtshof.de (covered by bundesgerichtshof.de scoping)

BMF-Schreiben PDFs live under bundesfinanzministerium.de/Content/DE/Downloads/BMF_Schreiben/ and index well by topic keywords; BaFin Rundschreiben and Merkblätter are under bafin.de/DE/Rechtsgrundlagen/

DPMA register lookups (DPMAregister) and BfArM/AMIce databases are separate query applications behind the main sites — reach the register interface directly (register.dpma.de is covered by dpma.de scoping) and use browser automation if the search form is JS-driven

name: United Kingdom
description: United Kingdom, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation (legislation.gov.uk), case law (Find Case Law at the National Archives and BAILII), the Supreme Court and the judiciary, both Houses of Parliament, and the regulators and departments — HMRC, FCA, Bank of England/PRA, CMA, ICO, IPO, MHRA, Companies House and the tribunals — across civil, criminal, commercial, employment, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP, health and immigration law
instructions: Cite primary legislation by short title and year with section (Companies Act 2006, s.172; Finance Act 2024, Sch. 1) from the consolidated text on legislation.gov.uk, and state whether you are reading the 'latest available' version (legislation.gov.uk carries unapplied-amendment warnings — check them); cite secondary legislation as SI year/number

Cite case law by neutral citation and court ([2024] UKSC 15; [2023] EWCA Civ 780; [2023] EWHC 1122 (Ch); [2024] UKUT 200 (TCC) for the Upper Tribunal Tax and Chancery Chamber) — full text is on caselaw.nationalarchives.gov.uk (Find Case Law, the official source) and bailii.org; the Supreme Court also publishes its own judgments and press summaries at supremecourt.uk

gov.uk is the single portal for most central-government departments and regulators — route by discipline within it: tax administration and the HMRC internal manuals (gov.uk/hmrc-internal-manuals) and Revenue & Customs Briefs → HMRC; competition and mergers → CMA; industrial property → the Intellectual Property Office (IPO); medicines and devices → MHRA; employment tribunal and EAT decisions (gov.uk/employment-tribunal-decisions); the Environment Agency and Companies House filing guidance also sit under gov.uk. Treat HMRC manuals as HMRC's interpretation, not law

Route the remaining regulators to their own domains: financial-services conduct and the FCA Handbook → fca.org.uk; prudential regulation and monetary policy → Bank of England/PRA (bankofengland.co.uk); data protection and the UK GDPR → ICO (ico.org.uk); health-technology appraisal and clinical guidance → NICE (nice.org.uk); the live companies register → find-and-update.company-information.service.gov.uk

Distinguish primary law, delegated legislation, and regulator soft law: FCA Handbook rules bind authorised firms while FCA guidance and HMRC manuals are interpretive; ICO guidance and NICE guidelines are not statute — label each and cite by its own reference (Handbook module e.g. COBS 9, ICO guidance title, NICE guideline NG/CG number)

The UK has three legal systems: this source covers UK-wide and England-and-Wales material. When a question turns on Scots law or Northern Ireland law (separate courts, and devolved competences under the Scotland/Wales/NI settlements) flag it and note the devolved legislature and courts; legislation.gov.uk does carry asps (Scottish) and Senedd Acts, so identify the enacting legislature

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Act/SI (legislation.gov.uk), (ii) judgment (neutral citation), (iii) regulator rulebook (FCA Handbook), (iv) departmental or regulator guidance (HMRC manual, ICO/NICE guidance) — and give the pinpoint (section, paragraph or Handbook rule) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: legislation.gov.uk is well indexed and server-rendered: every provision has a stable URL (/ukpga/2006/46/section/172) with '/latest' and point-in-time (…/2020-01-01) variants — always check the 'Changes to legislation' / unapplied-amendments banner before relying on the text

Find Case Law (caselaw.nationalarchives.gov.uk) is the official judgment archive with stable /id/<court>/<year>/<num> URLs and an open API; bailii.org is broader historically but explicitly blocks bots and bulk access ('a site for people, not bots') — prefer Find Case Law for fetching and reserve BAILII for older material, opening single documents only
parliament.uk and bills.parliament.uk return 403 to a bare curl but load in a browser and via WebFetch — use bills.parliament.uk to track a Bill's stages and gov.uk for Explanatory Notes

HMRC internal manuals are at gov.uk/hmrc-internal-manuals/<manual-name> and index well by manual code plus paragraph (e.g. CFM, BIM references); employment tribunal decisions have their own search at gov.uk/employment-tribunal-decisions

The FCA Handbook is a hyperlinked rulebook at handbook.fca.org.uk (covered by fca.org.uk scoping); cite by sourcebook and rule reference; bankofengland.co.uk returns no response to bare curl but loads via browser/WebFetch
Companies House data is at find-and-update.company-information.service.gov.uk with a public search and API — company number lands directly on the filing history

name: United States
description: United States (federal), all legal disciplines: the U.S. Code and Statutes at Large (govinfo.gov, uscode.house.gov), enacted and proposed legislation (congress.gov), federal regulations (eCFR, Federal Register), the Supreme Court and the federal judiciary, and the federal agencies — IRS and Tax Court, SEC, CFTC, Federal Reserve, OCC, FDIC, FTC, DOJ, USPTO, Copyright Office, DOL, NLRB, EEOC, EPA, FDA, HHS, CFPB and USCIS — across constitutional, civil, criminal, commercial, labour, tax, securities, banking, antitrust, IP, environment, health, consumer-finance and immigration law
instructions: Cite statutes by U.S. Code title and section (26 U.S.C. § 61; 15 U.S.C. § 78j(b)) from the official U.S. Code at uscode.house.gov or govinfo.gov, and cite public laws by Pub. L. number and Statutes at Large (Pub. L. 117-169, 136 Stat. 1818); use congress.gov for the legislative history and status of bills

Cite federal regulations by CFR title and section from the eCFR (ecfr.gov), which is the continuously updated codification (note it is unofficial for citation but current), and cite rulemakings and notices by Federal Register citation (89 Fed. Reg. 12345) from federalregister.gov; docket materials are on regulations.gov

Cite case law by reporter or docket: Supreme Court opinions (slip and bound) at supremecourt.gov; federal appellate and district opinions via the courts at uscourts.gov (PACER is the docket system; free opinions are posted by each court); U.S. Tax Court opinions at ustaxcourt.gov (regular vs. memorandum vs. summary opinions carry different precedential weight)

Route each regulatory question to its agency: federal tax administration and guidance → IRS (irs.gov: Revenue Rulings, Revenue Procedures, Notices, and the Internal Revenue Manual — sub-regulatory guidance, not law); securities → SEC (sec.gov); derivatives → CFTC (cftc.gov); monetary policy and bank holding companies → Federal Reserve (federalreserve.gov); national banks → OCC (occ.gov); deposit insurance and state banks → FDIC (fdic.gov); antitrust and consumer protection → FTC (ftc.gov) and DOJ (justice.gov); patents and trade marks → USPTO (uspto.gov); copyright → Copyright Office (copyright.gov)

Route the remaining disciplines: labour standards → DOL (dol.gov); collective bargaining and unfair labor practices → NLRB (nlrb.gov); employment discrimination → EEOC (eeoc.gov); environment → EPA (epa.gov); food and drugs → FDA (fda.gov); health programs and HIPAA → HHS (hhs.gov); consumer financial products → CFPB (consumerfinance.gov); immigration benefits → USCIS (uscis.gov, with removal proceedings before DOJ/EOIR)

Distinguish the hierarchy of authority: Constitution and statutes → legislative rules (notice-and-comment regulations in the CFR, which bind) → sub-regulatory guidance (IRS Notices, agency bulletins, manuals, FAQs, no-action letters — persuasive, not binding, and post-Loper Bright entitled only to Skidmore weight); label each source by type and note its force

This source is the federal level. State and local law — most contract, property, tort, family, and much criminal and consumer law — is outside its scope; when a question turns on state law, flag it and note the relevant state code and courts are not covered here

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) statute (U.S.C./Pub. L.), (ii) regulation (CFR/Fed. Reg.), (iii) judicial opinion, (iv) sub-regulatory agency guidance — and give the pinpoint (section, subsection or paragraph) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: govinfo.gov is the authoritative full-text repository (U.S. Code, Statutes at Large, Federal Register, CFR, court opinions from the U.S. Courts Opinions collection) with stable content-detail URLs and a documented API — prefer it for citable PDFs; the eCFR (ecfr.gov) is better for the current consolidated regulation and offers point-in-time views

Several federal sites refuse the sandbox's automated fetch (congress.gov, sec.gov, dol.gov, hhs.gov and consumerfinance.gov return 403; uscode.house.gov and nlrb.gov refuse the connection; fda.gov apex 404s while www.fda.gov serves) — these are firewall/CDN blocks of non-browser clients, not dead domains: reach the specific document URL, use govinfo.gov as a mirror for statutes/regulations, or fall back to browser automation

congress.gov is the canonical source for bill text, status, and Congressional Record; search by bill number (e.g. 'H.R. 1 118th Congress') — the site is JS-assisted and blocks bare fetches, so use the browser or its API

IRS sub-regulatory guidance (Revenue Rulings, Notices, the Internal Revenue Manual) is under irs.gov/irb (Internal Revenue Bulletin) and irs.gov/irm; the Tax Court posts opinions at ustaxcourt.gov/case_search under its docket system

Federal court opinions: supremecourt.gov/opinions serves slip opinions by term; individual circuit and district courts post opinions on uscourts.gov sub-sites, but comprehensive free-text search of dockets requires PACER (paid) — for free case-law search a secondary database (e.g. CourtListener) is often the practical entry point, then verify against the official opinion
eCFR and Federal Register both expose clean per-section/per-document URLs and APIs (ecfr.gov/current/title-26/... ; federalregister.gov/documents/YYYY/MM/DD/...) and index well by citation

name: Italy
description: Italy, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation (Normattiva) and the Gazzetta Ufficiale, the Constitutional Court, the Court of Cassation (civil and criminal), the administrative justice system (Consiglio di Stato and TAR), the Court of Auditors, both chambers of parliament (Camera, Senato) and the state authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, labour and social, administrative, tax, financial, competition, communications, data protection, IP, environment, health and immigration law
instructions: For any discipline the primary norm is the consolidated text on Normattiva (normattiva.it): cite statutes by type, number and date (d.lgs. 231/2001, l. 212/2000, art. 2043 c.c.) and prefer the multivigenza (version-in-force) text, stating its date; cite enactments by their Gazzetta Ufficiale publication (GU Serie Generale n., date). Ministry and authority pages are secondary guidance, not statute

Route each question to its court: constitutionality → Corte costituzionale (cortecostituzionale.it, sentenze cited n./year, e.g. Corte cost. n. 20/2025); civil and criminal → Corte di cassazione (cortedicassazione.it, cited Cass. civ./pen., sez., date, n.); administrative → Consiglio di Stato and the TAR via giustizia-amministrativa.it (Cons. Stato, sez., n./year); accounting and public-finance liability → Corte dei conti (corteconti.it)

For tax, distinguish binding law (Normattiva/GU) from the Agenzia delle Entrate interpretive practice — circolari, risoluzioni and risposte a interpello (agenziaentrate.gov.it) bind the administration, not the taxpayer or the courts; tax litigation runs before the Corti di giustizia tributaria (giustiziatributaria.gov.it) and the Cassazione sezione tributaria; the Dipartimento delle Finanze doctrine is at def.finanze.it

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: securities and markets → CONSOB (consob.it); banking and monetary supervision → Banca d'Italia (bancaditalia.it); insurance → IVASS (ivass.it); competition and consumer protection (unfair commercial practices) → AGCM (agcm.it); electronic communications and media → AGCOM (agcom.it); data protection → Garante per la protezione dei dati personali (garanteprivacy.it); industrial property (patents, trade marks, designs) → UIBM (uibm.mise.gov.it); anti-corruption and public procurement → ANAC (anticorruzione.it)

For labour and social matters distinguish ministry policy (lavoro.gov.it) from the social-security and accident-insurance institutions (INPS at inps.it, INAIL at inail.it) and the labour section of the ordinary courts; for environment cite the codice dell'ambiente on Normattiva and ministry guidance (MASE, mase.gov.it); for health and medicines cite the Ministero della Salute (salute.gov.it) and AIFA (aifa.gov.it); immigration → interno.gov.it

Distinguish binding law from interpretive positions: Agenzia delle Entrate circolari/risoluzioni/interpelli, Banca d'Italia and CONSOB regolamenti/comunicazioni, and Garante provvedimenti are administrative or regulatory acts of varying force — label each by type, number and date and state whether it is a regulation, a sanction or interpretive guidance

Italy is a regional state: this source is the national level. When a question turns on regional (Regione) competence — much of health administration, territory, commerce — flag it and note the relevant Bollettino Ufficiale Regionale and regional TAR are outside this source's scope

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) legge/decreto (Normattiva/GU), (ii) sentenza, (iii) administrative practice (circolare, risoluzione, provvedimento), (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (articolo, massima or paragrafo) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: Most Italian government and authority sites sit behind a WAF that returns no response (or a challenge) to a bare curl request while loading normally in a browser and via WebFetch — normattiva.it, gazzettaufficiale.it, giustizia-amministrativa.it, corteconti.it, consob.it, bancaditalia.it, ivass.it, agcom.it, salute.gov.it and anticorruzione.it among them: fetch the specific document URL or use browser automation rather than crawling from the landing page

Normattiva (normattiva.it) is server-rendered but its permalinks are generated (atto/vediMenuHTML with URN:NIR identifiers); the cleanest path is to search the act by type/number/year in the on-site engine and open the multivigenza view, then cite the article at the chosen date-in-force
The Gazzetta Ufficiale (gazzettaufficiale.it) serves each issue at /gazzetta/serie_generale/... ; use it for the promulgated text and Normattiva for the consolidated one

Corte costituzionale decisions are at cortecostituzionale.it/actionSchedaPronuncia (searchable by number/year); Cassazione full text is behind the Italgiure/CED database (accessible via cortedicassazione.it) which is credential/portal-gated — for a known massima the SentenzeWeb interface or a secondary reporter may be reachable first

The canonical UIBM host is uibm.mise.gov.it (under the Ministero delle Imprese e del Made in Italy); uibm.gov.it does not serve the site. Trade-mark and patent register searches run through separate UIBM/EUIPO query applications
Agenzia delle Entrate documents live under agenziaentrate.gov.it/portale/ and index by circolare/risoluzione number and year; interpelli are published as 'Risposte' with a running number per year

name: Netherlands
description: Netherlands, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation (wetten.overheid.nl) and the official gazettes (officielebekendmakingen.nl — Staatsblad, Staatscourant), case law (rechtspraak.nl), the Supreme Court (Hoge Raad) and the Council of State (Raad van State, highest administrative court and adviser), both chambers of parliament (Tweede en Eerste Kamer) and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, labour and social, administrative, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP, health and immigration law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text on wetten.overheid.nl: cite statutes by short title and article (art. 6:162 BW; art. 8:42 Awb; Wet IB 2001 art. 3.8), stating the version-in-force date; cite enactments in the Staatsblad and lower rules/notices in the Staatscourant via officielebekendmakingen.nl

Cite case law by ECLI (ECLI:NL:HR:2024:123; ECLI:NL:RVS:2023:...) from rechtspraak.nl; the Netherlands has no constitutional court and (until the 2023 reform) a limited constitutional-review prohibition — route by forum: civil, criminal and tax cassation → Hoge Raad (hogeraad.nl); highest general administrative appeal and advisory opinions → Raad van State (raadvanstate.nl); social-security and tax also reach the CRvB and the gerechtshoven

For tax, distinguish binding law from the Belastingdienst practice: beleidsbesluiten and the tax authority's guidance (belastingdienst.nl, policy set by the Ministerie van Financiën on rijksoverheid.nl) bind the administration, not the courts; tax litigation runs Rechtbank → Gerechtshof → Hoge Raad (belastingkamer)

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: conduct-of-business and markets → AFM (afm.nl); prudential supervision and monetary matters → De Nederlandsche Bank (dnb.nl); competition, consumer and sector regulation (energy, telecom, transport) → ACM (acm.nl); data protection → Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens (autoriteitpersoonsgegevens.nl); trade marks and designs → BOIP, the Benelux Office (boip.int); medicines → CBG-MEB (cbg-meb.nl); immigration and asylum → IND (ind.nl)

Distinguish binding law from interpretive positions: beleidsregels, AFM/DNB beleidsregels and Q&As, and AP guidance are administrative or supervisory positions, not statute — label each by type and date; the Netherlands transposes much EU law, so identify the implementing Dutch provision and the underlying directive/regulation

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) wet/AMvB (wetten.overheid.nl), (ii) uitspraak by ECLI, (iii) beleidsbesluit/beleidsregel, (iv) supervisory guidance — and give the pinpoint (artikel, rechtsoverweging) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: wetten.overheid.nl is server-rendered with stable BWB identifiers and point-in-time views (/BWBR.../YYYY-MM-DD) — search the citeertitel plus article; officielebekendmakingen.nl and zoek.officielebekendmakingen.nl hold the gazettes and parliamentary papers (Kamerstukken)
rechtspraak.nl serves each decision at deeplink.rechtspraak.nl/uitspraak?id=ECLI:NL:... and exposes an open data feed — an ECLI resolves directly to full text

raadvanstate.nl, dnb.nl and autoriteitpersoonsgegevens.nl return 403 to a bare fetch (WAF) but load in a browser and via WebFetch; hogeraad.nl similarly blocks curl — open the specific document URL or use browser automation
BOIP (boip.int) is the Benelux trade-mark/design office shared by NL, BE and LU — its register (register.boip.int) is a separate query application

name: Belgium
description: Belgium, all legal disciplines: the Belgisch Staatsblad/Moniteur belge and consolidated legislation (Justel on ejustice.just.fgov.be), the Constitutional Court, the case-law portal Juportal (Cour de cassation and lower courts), the Council of State, both chambers of parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, labour and social, administrative, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and economic law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated Justel text and the Belgisch Staatsblad/Moniteur belge on ejustice.just.fgov.be: cite codes and statutes by article and title in both language versions where relevant (art. 1382 oud BW / art. 5.1 nieuw BW; art. 24 WIB92 / CIR92), and cite enactments by their Moniteur publication date

Route by court: constitutionality and conflicts of competence between federal state, communities and regions → Grondwettelijk Hof / Cour constitutionnelle (const-court.be, arrest n./year); civil, criminal and commercial cassation and most published case law → Juportal (juportal.be); administrative annulment and advice on draft legislation → Raad van State / Conseil d'État (raadvst-consetat.be)

For tax, distinguish binding law from the administration's doctrine: the FOD/SPF Financiën guidance and circulaires are published on fisconetplus.be and financien.belgium.be and bind the administration, not the courts; rulings come from the Dienst Voorafgaande Beslissingen (ruling commission)

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: financial markets, conduct and insurance → FSMA (fsma.be); prudential supervision and monetary matters → Nationale Bank / Banque nationale (nbb.be); competition → Belgische Mededingingsautoriteit / Autorité belge de la concurrence (bma-abc.be); data protection → Gegevensbeschermingsautoriteit / APD (gegevensbeschermingsautoriteit.be); IP, consumer and market regulation → FOD/SPF Economie (economie.fgov.be); labour and social law → werk.belgie.be

Belgium is a federal state with communities and regions: this source is chiefly the federal level. When a question turns on regional or community competence (environment, spatial planning, much of economic and employment policy, taxation devolved to the regions) flag it and note the regional gazette/parliament, though the Moniteur belge does publish regional decrees too

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) wet/decreet (Justel/Moniteur), (ii) arrest/vonnis (Juportal, const-court), (iii) circulaire/ruling, (iv) regulator guidance — and give the pinpoint (artikel/considerans) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: ejustice.just.fgov.be holds both the Moniteur belge (…/mopdf/…) and the consolidated Justel database (…/eli/… and numac identifiers) — search by numac number or ELI; results and text are bilingual
Juportal (juportal.be) replaced Jure/Juridat as the official case-law portal; the Constitutional Court also serves arresten at const-court.be with stable numbering

fisconetplus.be (the tax documentation database) is a JS/portal application — for a known circulaire reference use its number, and fall back to browser automation if the search form does not respond to a plain fetch
Bare curl generally succeeds on Belgian federal sites, but the case-law and fisconet portals are query-driven — reach specific documents by identifier rather than crawling

name: Luxembourg
description: Luxembourg, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation and the Journal officiel (Legilux), the justice portal (ordinary and administrative courts), the Chamber of Deputies and the Council of State, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, labour, administrative, tax, financial, competition and data-protection law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text on Legilux (legilux.public.lu): cite laws and règlements grand-ducaux by date and title, preferring the texte coordonné and its ELI, and cite publications in the Journal officiel (Mémorial A) by number and date

Route by court: civil, criminal and commercial → the ordinary courts (Cour supérieure de justice) via justice.public.lu; administrative litigation → the Tribunal administratif and Cour administrative (justice.public.lu); the Conseil d'État (conseil-etat.public.lu) gives avis on draft legislation (its former judicial role ended in 1996)

For tax, distinguish binding law from doctrine: direct taxes → Administration des contributions directes (impotsdirects.public.lu), which issues circulaires L.I.R. binding on the administration; registration, VAT and estates → Administration de l'enregistrement, des domaines et de la TVA (aed.public.lu); guichet.public.lu carries authoritative administrative procedure information

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: the financial sector (banks, funds, investment firms) → CSSF (cssf.lu), whose circulaires bind supervised entities; monetary matters → Banque centrale du Luxembourg (bcl.lu); competition → Autorité de la concurrence (concurrence.public.lu); data protection → CNPD (cnpd.public.lu)

Distinguish binding law from interpretive positions: ACD circulaires, CSSF circulaires/FAQ and CNPD guidance are administrative or supervisory positions, not statute — label each by type and date; Luxembourg is a major fund and holding jurisdiction, so identify the CSSF circular and the EU directive/regulation behind a supervisory requirement

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) loi/règlement (Legilux), (ii) jugement/arrêt, (iii) circulaire (ACD, CSSF), (iv) supervisory guidance — and give the pinpoint (article, considérant) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: Legilux (legilux.public.lu) serves consolidated texts (eli/etat/leg/code/...) and the Journal officiel (eli/etat/leg/loi/...); search by ELI or by date/title — texts are in French
CSSF circulaires are listed at cssf.lu/en/regulatory-framework/ by number (e.g. CSSF 12/552) and are the practical core of Luxembourg financial supervision

justice.public.lu aggregates the ordinary and administrative jurisdictions; administrative case law (jurad) is searchable there, but many first-instance judgments are not published — cite by court, date and role number
Most .public.lu and .lu sites answer a plain fetch; portal search forms may still need the browser for query results

name: Switzerland
description: Switzerland (federal), all legal disciplines: consolidated federal law and the official compilations (Fedlex — SR, AS, Bundesblatt), the Federal Supreme Court and the specialised federal courts (administrative, criminal, patent), the Federal Assembly, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP, health and immigration law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text on Fedlex (fedlex.admin.ch): cite by Systematic Compilation (SR) number and article (art. 41 OR / SR 220; art. 18 DBG / SR 642.11), and cite enactments and messages via the Amtliche Sammlung (AS) and Bundesblatt (BBl) on Fedlex; give the language version relied on (DE/FR/IT are equally authentic)

Route by court: nearly all final appeals — civil, criminal, public-law, tax, social-insurance — go to the Bundesgericht / Tribunal fédéral (bger.ch), cited by ATF/BGE volume and page or by docket (e.g. BGE 149 II 147; 9C_123/2024); federal administrative first instance → Bundesverwaltungsgericht (bvger.ch); federal criminal → Bundesstrafgericht (bstger.ch); patents → Bundespatentgericht

For tax, distinguish binding law from practice: the Eidgenössische Steuerverwaltung (estv.admin.ch) issues Kreisschreiben and circulaires that bind the administration; note the federal/cantonal split — direct taxes are largely cantonal, harmonised by the StHG, so cantonal law and practice often govern

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: banks, insurers, markets → FINMA (finma.ch), whose Rundschreiben state supervisory practice; monetary policy → Swiss National Bank (snb.ch); competition → WEKO/COMCO (weko.admin.ch); data protection → EDÖB/PFPDT (edoeb.admin.ch); patents, trade marks, designs → IGE/IPI (ige.ch); therapeutic products → Swissmedic (swissmedic.ch); migration → SEM (sem.admin.ch)

Switzerland is federal with strong cantonal autonomy: this source is the federal level. When a question turns on cantonal competence (much of tax, civil procedure enforcement, police, health, construction) flag it and note the cantonal law and courts are outside this source's scope

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Gesetz/Verordnung (Fedlex SR), (ii) Urteil (BGE/docket), (iii) Kreisschreiben/circulaire, (iv) FINMA-Rundschreiben — and give the pinpoint (Artikel, Erwägung) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: Fedlex (fedlex.admin.ch) serves each act at a stable SR URL (/eli/cc/.../de) with point-in-time versions and all three language versions — search the SR number or the abbreviation plus article; it superseded the old admin.ch/ch/d/sr links
Bundesgericht leading decisions (BGE/ATF) are at bger.ch/ext/... and relevancy.bger.ch; search by BGE citation or docket number — non-leading decisions are published anonymised
bvger.ch and bstger.ch publish their decisions with their own docket schemes; sem.admin.ch returns no response to bare curl but loads via browser/WebFetch
FINMA Rundschreiben are listed at finma.ch/en/documentation/circulars/ by number and year

name: Ireland
description: Ireland, all legal disciplines: consolidated and enacted legislation (Irish Statute Book), the courts and their judgments, the Oireachtas, and the authorities — Revenue, Central Bank, CCPC, DPC, IPOI, CRO, WRC and Labour Court, HPRA — across constitutional, civil, criminal, commercial, employment, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP, company, health and immigration law
instructions: Cite primary legislation by short title and year with section (Companies Act 2014, s.212; Taxes Consolidation Act 1997, s.110) from the Irish Statute Book (irishstatutebook.ie), using the Revised Acts (consolidated) where available and noting the amendment position; cite statutory instruments as S.I. No. of year

Cite case law by neutral citation and court ([2024] IESC 12; [2023] IECA 145; [2023] IEHC 300) — judgments are on courts.ie and also on BAILII; the Supreme Court and Court of Appeal sit above the High Court, with constitutional review exercised by the ordinary courts (no separate constitutional court)

For tax, distinguish binding law from Revenue's interpretation: Revenue guidance, Tax and Duty Manuals and eBriefs (revenue.ie) state the administration's position and are not law; tax appeals go to the Tax Appeals Commission and then the courts

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: financial services, banking, insurance and markets → Central Bank of Ireland (centralbank.ie), the single regulator; competition and consumer protection → CCPC (ccpc.ie); data protection and the GDPR (Ireland is lead authority for many big-tech firms) → Data Protection Commission (dataprotection.ie); IP → Intellectual Property Office of Ireland (ipoi.gov.ie); company and beneficial-ownership filings → CRO (cro.ie)

Route employment and health: employment rights adjudication → Workplace Relations Commission (workplacerelations.ie) with appeals to the Labour Court (labourcourt.ie); medicines and devices → HPRA (hpra.ie); immigration and visas → irishimmigration.ie; the Oireachtas (oireachtas.ie) carries Bills, debates and Acts as passed

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Act/S.I. (Irish Statute Book), (ii) judgment (neutral citation), (iii) regulator rulebook (Central Bank), (iv) guidance (Revenue manual, DPC guidance) — and give the pinpoint (section, paragraph) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: irishstatutebook.ie returns 403 to a bare fetch (WAF) but loads in a browser and via WebFetch; it offers Revised Acts (consolidated) and links each section — check whether a Revised Act reflects the latest amendments
courts.ie hosts judgments with a search by court and neutral citation; BAILII (bailii.org) mirrors Irish decisions but blocks bots — prefer courts.ie for fetching
Revenue Tax and Duty Manuals are at revenue.ie/en/tax-professionals/tdm/ and index by manual and paragraph; eBriefs announce changes
cro.ie and irishimmigration.ie return 403 to bare curl but load via browser/WebFetch; the CRO company search (core.cro.ie) is a separate application

name: Portugal
description: Portugal, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation and the Diário da República (DRE), the case-law databases (DGSI), the Constitutional Court, the Supreme Court of Justice and Supreme Administrative Court, the Assembly of the Republic, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, labour and social, administrative, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP, health and immigration law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text and the official gazette on the Diário da República (diariodarepublica.pt): cite statutes by type, number and date (Lei n.º 7/2009 — Código do Trabalho; DL n.º 442-B/88 — CIRC) and prefer the versão consolidada, stating its date; the DRE is the authoritative publication

Route by court: constitutionality → Tribunal Constitucional (tribunalconstitucional.pt, acórdão n./year); civil and criminal → Supremo Tribunal de Justiça (stj.pt); administrative and tax → Supremo Tribunal Administrativo (stadministrativo.pt); published case law of the higher courts is aggregated in the DGSI databases (dgsi.pt)

For tax, distinguish binding law from doctrine: the Autoridade Tributária guidance — circulares, ofícios-circulados and informações vinculativas (portaldasfinancas.gov.pt) — binds the administration, not the courts; tax arbitration before CAAD and the administrative-tax courts resolves disputes

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: securities and markets → CMVM (cmvm.pt); banking and monetary matters → Banco de Portugal (bportugal.pt); insurance and pension funds → ASF (asf.com.pt); competition → Autoridade da Concorrência (concorrencia.pt); data protection → CNPD (cnpd.pt); industrial property → INPI (inpi.pt); labour inspection and conditions → ACT (act.gov.pt); medicines → INFARMED (infarmed.pt); immigration and asylum → AIMA (aima.gov.pt, successor to SEF)

Distinguish binding law from interpretive positions: AT circulares and informações vinculativas, Banco de Portugal/CMVM regulamentos and CNPD deliberações are administrative or regulatory acts of varying force — label each by type, number and date

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) lei/decreto-lei (DRE), (ii) acórdão (DGSI), (iii) doutrina administrativa (circular, informação vinculativa), (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (artigo, sumário) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: Most Portuguese government sites sit behind a WAF that gives no response to bare curl while loading in a browser and via WebFetch — dgsi.pt, tribunalconstitucional.pt, parlamento.pt, portaldasfinancas.gov.pt, cmvm.pt, bportugal.pt, asf.com.pt, concorrencia.pt, cnpd.pt, act.gov.pt and infarmed.pt among them: fetch the specific document URL or use browser automation
diariodarepublica.pt (DRE) serves consolidated texts and the gazette; use the dre.pt legislation-consolidada path and cite the versão consolidada with its date
DGSI (dgsi.pt) is the IGFEJ case-law portal aggregating STJ, TC, STA, Relação and other databases — each database has its own search; open a decision by its número de processo

The Portal das Finanças (portaldasfinancas.gov.pt) splits public information (info.portaldasfinancas.gov.pt) from the authenticated taxpayer area — cite doctrine from the public 'Informação Fiscal' section; the ASF domain is asf.com.pt (not asf.pt)

name: Austria
description: Austria, all legal disciplines: consolidated federal law and the Bundesgesetzblatt via the Rechtsinformationssystem (RIS), the Constitutional, Supreme, Administrative and specialised federal courts (VfGH, OGH, VwGH, BVwG, BFG), parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text in the RIS (ris.bka.gv.at): cite statutes by section and short title (§ 1295 ABGB; § 4 EStG) stating the version-in-force, and cite enactments by Bundesgesetzblatt reference (BGBl. I Nr./year), also published in RIS

Route by court: constitutionality → Verfassungsgerichtshof (vfgh.gv.at); civil and criminal → Oberster Gerichtshof (ogh.gv.at); general administrative review → Verwaltungsgerichtshof (vwgh.gv.at) over the Bundesverwaltungsgericht (bvwg.gv.at); tax and fiscal matters → Bundesfinanzgericht (bfg.gv.at). All case law is also in the RIS Judikatur databases, cited by court and Geschäftszahl

For tax, distinguish binding law from administrative guidance: BMF Richtlinien and Erlässe (bmf.gv.at, also in the RIS Findok) bind the administration, not the courts; the BFG decides appeals with onward review to the VwGH or VfGH

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: banks, insurers, securities and markets → FMA (fma.gv.at); monetary matters and banking analysis → Oesterreichische Nationalbank (oenb.at); competition → Bundeswettbewerbsbehörde (bwb.gv.at); data protection → Datenschutzbehörde (dsb.gv.at); patents, trade marks and designs → Patentamt (patentamt.at); medicines and devices → BASG/AGES (basg.gv.at)

Austria is a federal state: this source is the federal (Bund) level. When a question turns on Land competence flag it and note the Landesrecht consolidated in the RIS Landesrecht database and the Landesverwaltungsgerichte

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Gesetz/Verordnung (RIS/BGBl), (ii) Entscheidung (court + Geschäftszahl), (iii) Richtlinie/Erlass, (iv) supervisory guidance — and give the pinpoint (§, Randziffer) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: The RIS (ris.bka.gv.at) is the single authoritative portal — separate databases for Bundesrecht konsolidiert, BGBl, and Judikatur (Justiz/OGH, Vfgh, Vwgh, Bvwg, Bfg); it is well indexed and each norm/decision has a stable Dokumentnummer URL
BMF tax guidance is in RIS Findok (findok.bmf.gv.at) as well as bmf.gv.at — search by Richtlinie name (e.g. EStR) plus Randzahl
fma.gv.at returns 403 to bare curl and bwb.gv.at redirects (307) but both load via browser/WebFetch — reach specific documents directly
Court decisions are best retrieved via the RIS Judikatur search by Geschäftszahl rather than the individual court sites

name: Sweden
description: Sweden, all legal disciplines: the statute book (SFS) and legal portal (riksdagen.se, svenskforfattningssamling.se, lagrummet.se), the courts and their judgments (Högsta domstolen, Högsta förvaltningsdomstolen), parliament and government, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, consumer, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: Cite statutes by SFS number and chapter/section (SFS 1962:700 Brottsbalken 3 kap. 1 §; Inkomstskattelagen 1999:1229) — the consolidated SFS is at svenskforfattningssamling.se and riksdagen.se; preparatory works (förarbeten: propositioner, SOU) matter greatly in Swedish interpretation and are on riksdagen.se and regeringen.se

Cite case law by court and case number: Högsta domstolen precedents as NJA year s. page; Högsta förvaltningsdomstolen (tax and administrative) as HFD year ref. number — judgments and court information are via domstol.se

For tax, distinguish binding law from Skatteverket's position: Skatteverket's ställningstaganden, allmänna råd and guidance (skatteverket.se) state the agency's view and bind the agency, not the courts; tax cases run through the förvaltningsrätt → kammarrätt → HFD

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: financial supervision and markets → Finansinspektionen (fi.se); monetary policy → Riksbanken (riksbank.se); competition and public procurement → Konkurrensverket (konkurrensverket.se); consumer protection → Konsumentverket (konsumentverket.se); data protection → IMY (imy.se); patents, trade marks and designs → PRV (prv.se); medicines → Läkemedelsverket (lakemedelsverket.se)

Distinguish binding law from soft law: myndighetsföreskrifter (agency regulations, e.g. FFFS from Finansinspektionen) bind, while allmänna råd and vägledningar are advisory — label each and cite by its own series and number

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) lag/förordning (SFS), (ii) prejudikat (NJA/HFD), (iii) myndighetsföreskrift, (iv) ställningstagande/allmänt råd — and give the pinpoint (kapitel, paragraf) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: riksdagen.se hosts the SFS (Svensk författningssamling) in consolidated form plus all förarbeten (propositioner, betänkanden) — the single richest entry point; lagrummet.se is the public legal portal linking authorities' föreskrifter and case law
svenskforfattningssamling.se is the official electronic SFS since 2018 (the point of authenticity) and returns 403 to bare curl but loads via browser/WebFetch
skatteverket.se blocks bare curl but loads normally; its ställningstaganden and Rättslig vägledning (rattsligvagledning.skatteverket.se) are the practical tax doctrine
domstol.se provides court information and selected judgments; HFD and HD publish their referat/prejudikat there and in the annual reports

name: Denmark
description: Denmark, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation (Retsinformation) and Lovtidende, the courts and the judgments database, the Folketing, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, consumer, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text on Retsinformation (retsinformation.dk): cite statutes by their lovbekendtgørelse number and section (§ 33 A i kildeskatteloven; straffeloven § 279) and cite promulgated acts in Lovtidende (lovtidende.dk); preparatory works (lovforslag with bemærkninger) on ft.dk are central to interpretation

Cite case law by publication and court: Supreme Court and other judgments as U (Ugeskrift for Retsvæsen) year page, or via the official Domsdatabasen (domsdatabasen.dk); court information is on domstol.dk

For tax, distinguish binding law from the tax authority's practice: Skatteforvaltningen guidance is codified in Den juridiske vejledning (on skat.dk / info.skat.dk) and binds the administration; binding rulings (bindende svar) come from Skatterådet, and the Ministry of Taxation policy is on skm.dk; appeals run through Landsskatteretten to the courts

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: financial supervision and markets → Finanstilsynet (finanstilsynet.dk); monetary matters → Nationalbanken (nationalbanken.dk); competition and consumer → Konkurrence- og Forbrugerstyrelsen (kfst.dk); data protection → Datatilsynet (datatilsynet.dk); patents, trade marks and designs → Patent- og Varemærkestyrelsen (dkpto.dk); medicines → Lægemiddelstyrelsen (laegemiddelstyrelsen.dk)

Distinguish binding law from soft law: bekendtgørelser (executive orders) bind, while vejledninger and the tax Juridiske vejledning are administrative guidance — label each and cite by number and date

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) lov/bekendtgørelse (Retsinformation), (ii) dom (U-reference/Domsdatabasen), (iii) bindende svar, (iv) styrelse guidance — and give the pinpoint (paragraf, stykke) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: retsinformation.dk is the authoritative consolidated database with stable eli/... and document IDs; search the lovbekendtgørelse or the popular name plus paragraph
Domsdatabasen (domsdatabasen.dk) is the official free judgments database (rolled out from 2022) — older key decisions are cited from Ugeskrift for Retsvæsen (U)
domstol.dk and ft.dk return 403 to bare curl but load via browser/WebFetch; ft.dk carries the full lovforslag with remarks
Den juridiske vejledning (the tax authority's consolidated guidance) is the practical tax reference, updated twice yearly, on skat.dk

name: Norway
description: Norway, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation and case law (Lovdata), the Storting and government, the courts, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, consumer, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text on Lovdata (lovdata.no): cite statutes by short title and section (straffeloven § 271; skatteloven § 2-1) stating the version-in-force; preparatory works (forarbeider: Prop., NOU) on regjeringen.no and stortinget.no are central to Norwegian interpretation

Cite case law by Høyesterett reference (Rt. year page for older, HR-year-number-letter for recent, e.g. HR-2024-123-A) — Supreme Court and other judgments are on Lovdata (the free tier covers primary law and selected case law) and via domstol.no

For tax, distinguish binding law from the tax administration's practice: Skatteetaten guidance, the Skatte-ABC and bindende forhåndsuttalelser (skatteetaten.no) state the agency's position and bind the agency, not the courts

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: financial supervision (banking, insurance, markets) → Finanstilsynet (finanstilsynet.no); monetary matters → Norges Bank (norges-bank.no); competition → Konkurransetilsynet (konkurransetilsynet.no); consumer protection → Forbrukertilsynet (forbrukertilsynet.no); data protection → Datatilsynet (datatilsynet.no); patents, trade marks and designs → Patentstyret (patentstyret.no); medicines → Direktoratet for medisinske produkter (dmp.no)
Norway is in the EEA but not the EU: identify where a rule implements EEA-incorporated EU law, and note that EFTA Court case law and ESA guidance can bear on interpretation

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) lov/forskrift (Lovdata), (ii) dom (Rt./HR-reference), (iii) forhåndsuttalelse, (iv) tilsyn guidance — and give the pinpoint (paragraf, ledd) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: lovdata.no is the authoritative source: the free (åpne) tier serves consolidated statutes/forskrifter and Supreme Court decisions with stable NL/ document IDs; the paid Lovdata Pro has fuller case law — cite from the free tier where possible
regjeringen.no holds the forarbeider (Prop., Meld., NOU) and returns 403 to bare curl but loads via browser/WebFetch; stortinget.no tracks Bills
Skatteetaten's Skatte-ABC and Skatteforvaltningshåndboken are the practical tax references on skatteetaten.no
The medicines agency moved to dmp.no (Direktoratet for medisinske produkter, formerly Legemiddelverket / legemiddelverket.no) in 2024

name: Finland
description: Finland, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation, case law and the gazette (Finlex), parliament and government, the courts (Korkein oikeus, Korkein hallinto-oikeus), and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, consumer, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text on Finlex (finlex.fi): cite statutes by number/year and section (Rikoslaki 39/1889; Tuloverolaki 1535/1992, 29 §) in the version-in-force; Finlex also holds the säädöskokoelma (statute series), translations, and case law; government bills (HE) on eduskunta.fi and valtioneuvosto.fi are central to interpretation
Cite case law by court and yearbook number: Korkein oikeus as KKO year:number; Korkein hallinto-oikeus (tax and administrative) as KHO year:number — published on Finlex and via oikeus.fi

For tax, distinguish binding law from the tax administration's guidance: Verohallinto's ohjeet (syvennetyt vero-ohjeet) and ennakkoratkaisut/binding rulings (vero.fi) state the agency's position; appeals go through the hallinto-oikeus to the KHO

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: financial supervision and markets → Finanssivalvonta / FIN-FSA (finanssivalvonta.fi); monetary matters → Suomen Pankki (suomenpankki.fi); competition and consumer → Kilpailu- ja kuluttajavirasto / KKV (kkv.fi); data protection → Tietosuojavaltuutettu (tietosuoja.fi); patents, trade marks, companies → PRH (prh.fi); medicines → Fimea (fimea.fi)
Distinguish binding law from soft law: viranomaismääräykset (agency regulations, e.g. FIN-FSA standards) bind, while ohjeet and suositukset are guidance — label each and cite by number

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) laki/asetus (Finlex), (ii) ennakkopäätös (KKO/KHO), (iii) viranomaismääräys, (iv) ohje — and give the pinpoint (pykälä/momentti) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: Finlex (finlex.fi) is the authoritative free portal — consolidated legislation (ajantasainen), statute series, case law (oikeuskäytäntö) and some English translations; search the säädösnumero (number/year) or the act name plus section

eduskunta.fi returns 403 to bare curl and valtioneuvosto.fi/fimea.fi/tietosuoja.fi may block bare fetches, but all load via browser/WebFetch; government bills (HE number/year) are the key travaux préparatoires
Verohallinto's detailed guidance (syventävät vero-ohjeet) at vero.fi is the practical tax reference and is issued with a diaarinumero
FIN-FSA regulations and guidelines (määräykset ja ohjeet) are listed at finanssivalvonta.fi by number/year

name: Poland
description: Poland, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation (ISAP) and the Dziennik Ustaw, the Constitutional Tribunal, the Supreme Court and Supreme Administrative Court, both chambers of parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, consumer, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text (tekst jednolity) in ISAP (isap.sejm.gov.pl): cite statutes by title and article with the Dziennik Ustaw reference (art. 415 k.c.; ustawa o CIT, Dz.U. year poz. number); the promulgated text is in the Dziennik Ustaw (dziennikustaw.gov.pl)

Route by court: constitutionality → Trybunał Konstytucyjny (trybunal.gov.pl, sygn. K/P/SK number); civil and criminal cassation and legal-question resolutions → Sąd Najwyższy (sn.pl); administrative and tax → Naczelny Sąd Administracyjny and the WSA (nsa.gov.pl, orzeczenia.nsa.gov.pl), cited by sygnatura akt

For tax, distinguish binding law from the administration's interpretation: indywidualne interpretacje podatkowe and objaśnienia issued by the Krajowa Informacja Skarbowa (via podatki.gov.pl) protect the taxpayer who relies on them but are not law; tax disputes reach the WSA and NSA

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: financial market supervision (banking, insurance, capital markets) → KNF (knf.gov.pl); monetary matters → Narodowy Bank Polski (nbp.pl); competition and consumer protection → UOKiK (uokik.gov.pl); data protection → UODO (uodo.gov.pl); patents, trade marks and designs → Urząd Patentowy (uprp.gov.pl); medicines → URPL (urpl.gov.pl)

Distinguish binding law from interpretive positions: KNF rekomendacje and stanowiska, UOKiK decyzje, and tax objaśnienia/interpretacje are administrative or supervisory acts of varying force — label each by type, number and date

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) ustawa/rozporządzenie (ISAP/Dz.U.), (ii) orzeczenie (sygn. akt), (iii) interpretacja indywidualna/objaśnienia, (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (artykuł, teza) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: ISAP (isap.sejm.gov.pl) provides consolidated texts (tekst ujednolicony) and links to the Dziennik Ustaw entries by year/poz.; it is the practical entry point for legislation
NSA/WSA case law is searchable at orzeczenia.nsa.gov.pl (covered by nsa.gov.pl scoping) by sygnatura; Supreme Court decisions are on sn.pl
senat.gov.pl and nbp.pl return 403 to bare curl but load via browser/WebFetch; sejm.gov.pl carries the legislative process (druki sejmowe)
Tax individual interpretations are searchable via the Eureka/SIP system linked from podatki.gov.pl — search by keyword or signature; each carries a sygnatura and date

name: Australia
description: Australia (federal/Commonwealth), all legal disciplines: the Federal Register of Legislation and case law (AustLII), the High Court and Federal Court, Parliament, and the authorities across constitutional, civil, criminal, commercial, tax, corporate, prudential, competition, privacy, IP and therapeutic-goods law
instructions: Cite Commonwealth statutes by short title, year and section from the Federal Register of Legislation (legislation.gov.au), e.g. Corporations Act 2001 (Cth) s.180; Income Tax Assessment Act 1997 (Cth), noting the compilation date; delegated legislation is registered there too

Cite case law by medium-neutral citation: High Court (hcourt.gov.au) as [2024] HCA 12; Federal Court (fedcourt.gov.au) as [2023] FCA / [2023] FCAFC — AustLII (austlii.edu.au) is the comprehensive free database for Commonwealth, State and Territory courts and legislation

For tax, the Australian Taxation Office (ato.gov.au) issues binding public and private rulings and non-binding guidance; distinguish a public ruling (binding on the Commissioner) from an ATO interpretative decision or guideline

Route regulatory questions: corporations, financial services and markets → ASIC (asic.gov.au); prudential regulation of banks, insurers and super → APRA (apra.gov.au); monetary policy → the RBA (rba.gov.au); competition and consumer → ACCC (accc.gov.au); privacy → OAIC (oaic.gov.au); patents, trade marks and designs → IP Australia (ipaustralia.gov.au); therapeutic goods → TGA (tga.gov.au)

Australia is a federation: this source is the Commonwealth level. When a question turns on State/Territory law (most crime, property, torts, and much of contract) flag it and note the State legislation and courts (on AustLII) are outside this Commonwealth source

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Act/instrument (Register of Legislation), (ii) judgment (neutral citation), (iii) ATO ruling, (iv) regulator guidance — and give the pinpoint (section, paragraph) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: legislation.gov.au serves official compilations with point-in-time versions; AustLII (austlii.edu.au) is the practical entry for case law across all Australian jurisdictions and blocks bare curl but loads via browser
Several sites (austlii.edu.au, fedcourt.gov.au, aph.gov.au, ato.gov.au, rba.gov.au) return 403/redirects to bare curl but load via browser — open the specific document
ATO rulings (TR, TD, private rulings) and guidance are on ato.gov.au and index by ruling number
The Administrative Review Tribunal (ART, which replaced the AAT in 2024) hears tax and other merits reviews

name: Canada
description: Canada (federal), all legal disciplines: consolidated federal statutes and regulations (Justice Laws) and the Canada Gazette, the Supreme Court and the federal courts (with CanLII for all Canadian case law), Parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, privacy, IP and securities law
instructions: Cite federal statutes and regulations by short title and section from the consolidated Justice Laws site (laws-lois.justice.gc.ca), e.g. Income Tax Act, RSC 1985, c.1 (5th Supp.), s.9; note the 'last amended' date; enactments appear in the Canada Gazette (gazette.gc.ca)

Cite case law by neutral citation and court: Supreme Court (scc-csc.ca) as 2024 SCC 12; Federal Court and Federal Court of Appeal (fct-cf.gc.ca) as 2023 FC / 2023 FCA; Tax Court (tcc-cci.gc.ca) as 2023 TCC — CanLII (canlii.org) is the comprehensive free database for federal and all provincial/territorial courts
For tax, the Canada Revenue Agency (under canada.ca) issues Income Tax Folios, Interpretation Bulletins and advance rulings — administrative positions, not law; the Tax Court hears appeals

Route regulatory questions: prudential regulation of banks and insurers → OSFI (osfi-bsif.gc.ca); monetary policy → Bank of Canada (bankofcanada.ca); securities are provincial — the CSA (securities-administrators.ca) coordinates and provincial commissions such as the OSC (osc.ca) regulate; privacy (federal) → OPC (priv.gc.ca); patents, trade marks and copyright → CIPO (ised-isde.canada.ca); the Competition Bureau sits under canada.ca

Canada is federal and bijural (common law; civil law in Quebec): this source is the federal level. When a question turns on provincial law (most contract, property, tort, much of securities and consumer law, and Quebec civil law) flag it and note the provincial statutes and courts are outside this source's scope

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) statute/regulation (Justice Laws), (ii) judgment (neutral citation), (iii) CRA administrative guidance, (iv) regulator rule — and give the pinpoint (section, paragraph) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: laws-lois.justice.gc.ca is the official consolidation with point-in-time versions; CanLII (canlii.org) is the practical entry for case law across all jurisdictions and blocks bare curl but loads via browser
tcc-cci.gc.ca and fct-cf.gc.ca resolve on their www host and refuse bare curl — open the specific decision or use browser automation
CRA guidance (Folios, IT bulletins) is under canada.ca/en/revenue-agency; the Competition Bureau is under canada.ca/en/competition-bureau
Securities regulation is decentralised — start at securities-administrators.ca (CSA) then the relevant provincial commission (e.g. osc.ca)

name: Singapore
description: Singapore, all legal disciplines: consolidated statutes (Singapore Statutes Online), the Supreme Court and the judiciary, Parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP, company and health-products law
instructions: Cite statutes by short title and section from Singapore Statutes Online (sso.agc.gov.sg), e.g. Companies Act 1967 s.157; Income Tax Act 1947, using the current in-force version; Singapore is a common-law jurisdiction

Cite case law by neutral citation: [2024] SGCA 15; [2023] SGHC(A) / [2023] SGHC — judgments are on judiciary.gov.sg (Supreme Court) and curated at singaporelawwatch.sg; the Court of Appeal sits at the apex
For tax, the Inland Revenue Authority of Singapore (iras.gov.sg) issues e-Tax Guides and advance rulings; these state the Comptroller's interpretation

Route regulatory questions: banking, insurance, capital markets and monetary policy (integrated regulator and central bank) → MAS (mas.gov.sg); competition and consumer → CCCS (cccs.gov.sg); data protection (PDPA) → PDPC (pdpc.gov.sg); patents, trade marks and designs → IPOS (ipos.gov.sg); companies and business registration → ACRA (acra.gov.sg); health products → HSA (hsa.gov.sg)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Act/subsidiary legislation (SSO), (ii) judgment (neutral citation), (iii) IRAS e-Tax Guide/ruling, (iv) regulator guidance — and give the pinpoint (section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: sso.agc.gov.sg is the authoritative consolidated statutes site with point-in-time versions; it blocks bare curl but loads via browser
MAS (mas.gov.sg) is the single financial regulator and monetary authority — its Acts, regulations, notices and guidelines are all on mas.gov.sg
singaporelawwatch.sg curates recent judgments and links to the full text; the Supreme Court publishes on judiciary.gov.sg
IRAS e-Tax Guides index by topic on iras.gov.sg

name: Hong Kong
description: Hong Kong SAR, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation (Hong Kong e-Legislation) and case law (Judiciary, HKLII), the Legislative Council, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, securities, monetary, competition, data protection, IP and company law
