instructions: Cite ordinances by Cap. number and section from Hong Kong e-Legislation (elegislation.gov.hk), e.g. Companies Ordinance (Cap. 622) s.465; Inland Revenue Ordinance (Cap. 112), using the current in-force version; Hong Kong is a common-law jurisdiction under 'one country, two systems'

Cite case law by neutral citation: [2024] HKCFA 8; [2023] HKCA / [2023] HKCFI — judgments are on the Judiciary (judiciary.hk, legal reference system) and HKLII (hklii.hk); the Court of Final Appeal sits at the apex

For tax, the Inland Revenue Department (ird.gov.hk) issues Departmental Interpretation and Practice Notes (DIPNs) and advance rulings — administrative interpretation, not law; Hong Kong taxes on a territorial basis

Route regulatory questions: securities and futures → SFC (sfc.hk); banking and monetary matters → HKMA (hkma.gov.hk); competition → Competition Commission (compcomm.hk); data protection (PDPO) → PCPD (pcpd.org.hk); patents, trade marks and designs → Intellectual Property Department (ipd.gov.hk); companies → Companies Registry (cr.gov.hk)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Ordinance (e-Legislation), (ii) judgment (neutral citation), (iii) IRD DIPN/ruling, (iv) regulator code/guidance — and give the pinpoint (section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: elegislation.gov.hk is the authoritative bilingual consolidated legislation with point-in-time versions; it and judiciary.hk/hkma.gov.hk block bare curl but load via browser
HKLII (hklii.hk) and the Judiciary's legal reference system both host judgments; the CFA publishes its own decisions
SFC codes/guidelines are on sfc.hk; HKMA supervisory policy manual is on hkma.gov.hk
IRD DIPNs are on ird.gov.hk and index by number

name: Japan
description: Japan, all legal disciplines: consolidated laws (e-Gov Laws) and the courts (Supreme Court database), the Diet, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, monetary, competition, data protection, IP and pharmaceutical law
instructions: Cite statutes by law name, year/number and article from e-Gov Laws (laws.e-gov.go.jp), the official consolidated database, e.g. Civil Code (Minpo) art. 709; Corporation Tax Act (Hojinzeiho); enactments appear in the Kanpō (Official Gazette)
Cite case law by court, date and case type: Supreme Court (Saikō Saibansho) decisions are on courts.go.jp with a searchable database — cite by date and the Minshu/Keishu reporter where reported
For tax, the National Tax Agency (nta.go.jp) issues circulars (tsutatsu) and interpretive guidance binding the administration; the Ministry of Finance (mof.go.jp) sets tax policy

Route regulatory questions: banking, insurance, securities supervision → FSA (fsa.go.jp); monetary policy → Bank of Japan (boj.or.jp); competition → Japan Fair Trade Commission (jftc.go.jp); data protection (APPI) → Personal Information Protection Commission (ppc.go.jp); patents, trade marks and designs → JPO (jpo.go.jp); pharmaceuticals and devices → PMDA (pmda.go.jp)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) law/ordinance (e-Gov), (ii) court decision, (iii) NTA circular (tsutatsu), (iv) regulator guideline — and give the pinpoint (article) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: laws.e-gov.go.jp is the authoritative consolidated legislation with some English translations at japaneselawtranslation.go.jp; most Japanese government sites (courts.go.jp, nta.go.jp, fsa.go.jp, jpo.go.jp) resolve on their www host and block bare curl — use the browser
Supreme Court judgments are searchable at courts.go.jp (Hanrei database) by date and keyword
NTA circulars (tsutatsu) and interpretive Q&As are on nta.go.jp
FSA guidelines and JFTC guidelines are published in Japanese with selected English versions

name: China
description: China (mainland/PRC), all legal disciplines: the National Database of Laws and Regulations and government portal, the Supreme People's Court and its judgments database, the NPC, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, securities, monetary, banking/insurance, market-regulation/competition, IP, cybersecurity/data and drug law
instructions: Cite legislation by title and article from the National Database of Laws and Regulations (flk.npc.gov.cn), e.g. Civil Code (Minfadian) art. 1165; Enterprise Income Tax Law; the State Council portal (gov.cn) carries administrative regulations (xingzheng fagui) and departmental rules

Distinguish the hierarchy: laws (falü) enacted by the NPC (npc.gov.cn) → administrative regulations (State Council) → departmental rules (bumen guizhang) → judicial interpretations (sifa jieshi) of the Supreme People's Court, which are binding on courts; SPC guiding cases have de facto precedential weight
Cite case law from the Supreme People's Court (court.gov.cn) and China Judgements Online (wenshu.court.gov.cn), noting that publication of judgments has narrowed in recent years

Route regulatory questions: tax → State Taxation Administration (chinatax.gov.cn); securities → CSRC (csrc.gov.cn); monetary policy → PBoC (pbc.gov.cn); banking and insurance → National Financial Regulatory Administration (nfra.gov.cn, which replaced the CBIRC in 2023); market regulation, antitrust and consumer → SAMR (samr.gov.cn); IP → CNIPA (cnipa.gov.cn); cybersecurity, data and cross-border data → CAC (cac.gov.cn); drugs and devices → NMPA (nmpa.gov.cn)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) law/regulation/rule (flk.npc.gov.cn/gov.cn), (ii) judgment or judicial interpretation (SPC), (iii) departmental normative document, (iv) regulator guidance — and give the pinpoint (article) where the wording is decisive; note the distinction between binding rules and non-binding normative documents (guifanxing wenjian)
Hong Kong and Macau are separate legal systems under 'one country, two systems' — this source is mainland/PRC law only; use the Hong Kong or Macau source for those SARs
search_notes: flk.npc.gov.cn is the official consolidated legislation database; gov.cn carries State Council regulations and departmental rules — most PRC government sites resolve on www and block bare curl, so use the browser
wenshu.court.gov.cn (China Judgements Online) is the main case-law database, though its coverage has been reduced since 2021; SPC guiding cases are published separately
The NFRA (nfra.gov.cn) absorbed the former CBIRC's banking and insurance supervision in the 2023 reform
CAC (cac.gov.cn) governs data-export security assessments, PIPL enforcement and the cybersecurity review regime

name: India
description: India (Union), all legal disciplines: consolidated central legislation (India Code) and the Gazette, the Supreme Court and case law (Indian Kanoon), Parliament, and the authorities across constitutional, civil, criminal, commercial, tax, securities, monetary, competition, corporate and IP law
instructions: Cite central statutes by short title, year and section from the India Code (indiacode.nic.in), e.g. Companies Act 2013 s.149; Income-tax Act 1961 s.9; enactments appear in the Gazette of India (egazette.gov.in)

Cite case law by neutral/reporter citation: Supreme Court (sci.gov.in) as (2024) ... SCC ... or [2024] INSC ...; Indian Kanoon (indiankanoon.org) is the comprehensive free database for the Supreme Court and High Courts

For tax, distinguish law from administration: CBDT circulars (income tax, via incometax.gov.in) and CBIC circulars (GST/customs, cbic.gov.in) bind the department, not the courts; advance rulings and ITAT/tribunal decisions matter in disputes

Route regulatory questions: securities and markets → SEBI (sebi.gov.in); monetary policy, banking and FEMA → RBI (rbi.org.in); competition → CCI (cci.gov.in); companies and insolvency → MCA (mca.gov.in); patents, trade marks and designs → the IP Office (ipindia.gov.in); data protection under the DPDP Act 2023 is administered via MeitY pending the Data Protection Board — verify the operative rules
India is a federal union: this source is the Union level. When a question turns on State law (State GST, land, much of stamp duty and police) flag it and note the State legislation and High Courts

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Act/rule (India Code/Gazette), (ii) judgment (SCC/neutral citation), (iii) CBDT/CBIC circular, (iv) regulator (SEBI/RBI) regulation or circular — and give the pinpoint (section, para) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: indiacode.nic.in hosts consolidated central Acts with amendment history; the Supreme Court site is sci.gov.in (the older main.sci.gov.in no longer resolves)
Indian Kanoon (indiankanoon.org) is the practical free case-law search for the Supreme Court and High Courts and blocks bare curl but loads via browser
SEBI (sebi.gov.in) and RBI (rbi.org.in) publish regulations, master circulars and directions on their sites; incometax.gov.in and mca.gov.in block bare curl but load via browser
GST law (CGST/IGST and the GST Council) is on cbic.gov.in; State GST is separate

name: South Korea
description: South Korea, all legal disciplines: the Korea Law Information Center (consolidated law), the Supreme and Constitutional Courts, the National Assembly, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, monetary, competition, data protection, IP and drug-safety law
instructions: Cite statutes by name and article from the Korea Law Information Center (law.go.kr), the official consolidated database (with English translations), e.g. Civil Act art. 750; Corporate Tax Act; note the enforcement date

Route by court: constitutionality → Constitutional Court (ccourt.go.kr); civil, criminal and administrative cassation → Supreme Court (scourt.go.kr), whose precedents are searchable via glaw.scourt.go.kr; the National Assembly (assembly.go.kr) enacts law
For tax, the National Tax Service (nts.go.kr) issues authoritative interpretations (yuqwon haeseok) and rulings binding the administration; the Tax Tribunal hears appeals

Route regulatory questions: financial policy and licensing → FSC (fsc.go.kr) with supervision by the FSS (fss.or.kr); monetary policy → Bank of Korea (bok.or.kr); competition and consumer → KFTC (ftc.go.kr); data protection (PIPA) → PIPC (pipc.go.kr); patents, trade marks and designs → KIPO (kipo.go.kr); drugs and food safety → MFDS (mfds.go.kr)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) law/enforcement decree (law.go.kr), (ii) precedent (Supreme/Constitutional Court), (iii) NTS interpretation, (iv) regulator guideline — and give the pinpoint (article) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: law.go.kr is the authoritative consolidated legislation with English versions; glaw.scourt.go.kr (covered by scourt.go.kr) is the case-law search
The FSC (policy) and FSS (supervision) are distinct — route licensing/regulation to fsc.go.kr and supervisory practice to fss.or.kr
NTS authoritative interpretations are on nts.go.kr; the Tax Tribunal and NTS both publish rulings
KFTC and PIPC guidelines are on ftc.go.kr and pipc.go.kr

name: Brazil
description: Brazil (federal), all legal disciplines: consolidated federal legislation (Planalto) and the Diário Oficial da União, the Supreme Federal Court, the Superior Court of Justice and the Superior Labour Court, Congress, and the federal authorities across constitutional, civil, criminal, commercial, labour, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: Cite federal legislation by type/number/year and article from Planalto (planalto.gov.br), which hosts consolidated codes and laws (Lei nº 10.406/2002 — Código Civil, art. 927; Decreto-Lei; the Constituição Federal); the official gazette is the Diário Oficial da União (in.gov.br)

Route by court: constitutionality → Supremo Tribunal Federal (stf.jus.br, cited ADI/ADC/RE number); federal-law uniformity, civil and criminal → Superior Tribunal de Justiça (stj.jus.br, REsp number); labour → Tribunal Superior do Trabalho (tst.jus.br) — Brazil has a dedicated labour-court branch

For tax, distinguish binding law from administrative doctrine: Receita Federal soluções de consulta and instruções normativas (under gov.br/receitafederal) bind the administration; the administrative tax court CARF (gov.br/carf) decides federal tax assessments before the judiciary

Route regulatory questions: monetary matters and banking → Banco Central do Brasil (bcb.gov.br); securities and capital markets → CVM (cvm.gov.br); competition → CADE (cade.gov.br); data protection (LGPD) → ANPD (gov.br/anpd); industrial property → INPI (gov.br/inpi); health and medicines → ANVISA (gov.br/anvisa) — most federal agencies sit under the unified gov.br portal, routed by path

Brazil is a federation: this source is the federal level. When a question turns on state or municipal competence (ICMS, ISS, much of consumer and environmental enforcement) flag it and note the state legislation and tribunais de justiça are outside this source's scope

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) lei/decreto (Planalto/DOU), (ii) acórdão (STF/STJ/TST), (iii) solução de consulta/instrução normativa, (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (artigo, ementa) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: planalto.gov.br hosts the consolidated codes and the Constituição with stable /ccivil_03/ URLs; in.gov.br serves the DOU (search by date and section)
STF and STJ publish jurisprudence searchable by class and number; stf.jus.br blocks bare curl but loads via browser

Most federal agencies (Receita Federal, CARF, ANPD, INPI, ANVISA) live under gov.br/<agency> — the bare gov.br scope covers them; Banco Central (bcb.gov.br), CVM (cvm.gov.br) and CADE (cade.gov.br) keep their own domains
CARF administrative tax decisions are at gov.br/carf and index by acórdão number

name: Mexico
description: Mexico (federal), all legal disciplines: consolidated federal statutes (Cámara de Diputados, LeyesBiblio) and the Diario Oficial de la Federación, the Supreme Court and the federal judiciary, Congress, and the authorities across constitutional, civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, consumer, IP and health law
instructions: Cite federal legislation by name and article from the consolidated LeyesBiblio collection on diputados.gob.mx (Código Civil Federal, art. 1910; Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta) stating the last-reform date; the official gazette is the Diario Oficial de la Federación (dof.gob.mx)

Route by court: constitutional and final review (amparo, controversias, acciones de inconstitucionalidad) → Suprema Corte de Justicia de la Nación (scjn.gob.mx); federal judicial administration and circuit/district courts → Consejo de la Judicatura Federal (cjf.gob.mx); federal administrative and tax litigation → Tribunal Federal de Justicia Administrativa (tfja.gob.mx)

For tax, distinguish binding law from SAT criteria: the Servicio de Administración Tributaria (sat.gob.mx) issues the Resolución Miscelánea Fiscal (binding general rules) and criterios normativos; contested assessments go to the TFJA and then amparo

Route regulatory questions: banking, securities and financial entities → CNBV (cnbv.gob.mx); monetary matters → Banco de México (banxico.org.mx); competition → COFECE (cofece.mx); industrial property → IMPI (impi.gob.mx); consumer protection → PROFECO (profeco.gob.mx); health and medicines → COFEPRIS (gob.mx/cofepris)

Note the data-protection landscape is in transition: INAI was dissolved in 2025 and its functions reassigned within the executive — verify the current competent authority before relying on it; Mexico is a federation, so state codes (civil, criminal, procedural) govern many matters and are outside this federal source

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) ley/reglamento (LeyesBiblio/DOF), (ii) tesis/jurisprudencia (SCJN), (iii) Resolución Miscelánea/criterio (SAT), (iv) regulator rule — and give the pinpoint (artículo, tesis) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: diputados.gob.mx/LeyesBiblio serves the consolidated federal laws (PDF/Word) with reform history; dof.gob.mx is the gazette, searchable by date
SCJN jurisprudence and tesis are in the Semanario Judicial de la Federación (sjf.scjn.gob.mx, covered by scjn.gob.mx); many Mexican gov sites resolve on www and block bare curl — use the browser
SAT's Resolución Miscelánea Fiscal and criterios are on sat.gob.mx; the TFJA publishes precedentes on tfja.gob.mx
COFEPRIS and other agencies sit under gob.mx/<agency>

name: Argentina
description: Argentina (federal), all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation (InfoLEG, SAIJ) and the Boletín Oficial, the Supreme Court, Congress, and the authorities across constitutional, civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: Cite national legislation by law number and article from InfoLEG (servicios.infoleg.gob.ar) or SAIJ (saij.gob.ar), which hold consolidated texts (Código Civil y Comercial, art. 1716; Ley 20.628 de Impuesto a las Ganancias); the official gazette is the Boletín Oficial (boletinoficial.gob.ar)
Route by court: the Corte Suprema de Justicia de la Nación (csjn.gov.ar) is the constitutional and final court (fallos cited by tomo/página or by name); federal case law is also on SAIJ

For tax, the customs-and-revenue agency is now ARCA (arca.gob.ar), which replaced AFIP in 2024–2025: its dictámenes and resoluciones generales bind the administration; the tax hierarchy runs administrative determination → Tribunal Fiscal de la Nación → federal courts → CSJN

Route regulatory questions: securities and capital markets → CNV (cnv.gov.ar); monetary and banking matters → BCRA (bcra.gob.ar); competition, data protection (AAIP), consumer and medicines (ANMAT) sit under the unified argentina.gob.ar portal, routed by path; industrial property → INPI (inpi.gob.ar)

Argentina is a federation: this source is the national level. When a question turns on provincial competence (much procedure, gross-receipts tax 'ingresos brutos', local matters) flag it and note the provincial law and courts are outside this source's scope

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) ley/decreto (InfoLEG/SAIJ/Boletín Oficial), (ii) fallo (CSJN), (iii) resolución general/dictamen (ARCA), (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (artículo, considerando) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: InfoLEG (servicios.infoleg.gob.ar) and SAIJ (saij.gob.ar) both hold consolidated texts; SAIJ also carries jurisprudence and doctrine; most resolve on www and block bare curl — use the browser
The revenue agency migrated from AFIP (afip.gob.ar) to ARCA (arca.gob.ar) — use arca.gob.ar
AAIP (data protection), ANMAT (medicines) and the competition authority are under argentina.gob.ar/<dependencia>
CSJN fallos are searchable at csjn.gov.ar (Fallos and the sentencias database)

name: Chile
description: Chile, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation (LeyChile/BCN) and the Diario Oficial, the Constitutional Court and the Judiciary (Corte Suprema), Congress, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, transparency, IP and health law
instructions: Cite legislation by law number and article from LeyChile (leychile.cl, the Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional consolidated database), e.g. Código Civil, art. 2314; Ley sobre Impuesto a la Renta (DL 824), stating the version-in-force; the official gazette is the Diario Oficial (diariooficial.interior.gob.cl)

Route by court: constitutionality → Tribunal Constitucional (tribunalconstitucional.cl); ordinary civil, criminal and labour and the Corte Suprema → the Poder Judicial (pjud.cl), whose case law is searchable in its jurisprudence system

For tax, the Servicio de Impuestos Internos (sii.cl) issues circulares, oficios and resoluciones that bind the administration; tax litigation runs before the Tribunales Tributarios y Aduaneros and the Corte Suprema

Route regulatory questions: banking, securities, insurance and pensions (integrated regulator) → Comisión para el Mercado Financiero (cmfchile.cl); monetary matters → Banco Central (bcentral.cl); competition → Fiscalía Nacional Económica (fne.gob.cl) with the TDLC as tribunal; transparency and data (public) → Consejo para la Transparencia (consejotransparencia.cl); industrial property → INAPI (inapi.cl); medicines → ISP (ispch.gob.cl)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) ley/DL/DFL (LeyChile), (ii) sentencia (TC/Corte Suprema), (iii) circular/oficio (SII), (iv) regulator norm — and give the pinpoint (artículo, considerando) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: leychile.cl is the authoritative consolidated database with per-article versions and stable IDs; diariooficial.interior.gob.cl is the gazette
sii.cl, cmfchile.cl and bcentral.cl block bare curl but load via browser — SII circulares and oficios are indexed by number/year on sii.cl
Poder Judicial jurisprudence is searchable at pjud.cl (Buscador de jurisprudencia); the Tribunal Constitucional has its own sentencias search
The CMF is Chile's single financial-market regulator (it absorbed the former SBIF and SVS)

name: Colombia
description: Colombia, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation (SUIN-Juriscol) and the official gazette, the Constitutional Court, the Supreme Court and Council of State, Congress, and the authorities across constitutional, civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, consumer, company, IP and health law
instructions: Cite legislation by law number and article from SUIN-Juriscol (suin-juriscol.gov.co) or the consolidated codes on secretariasenado.gov.co (Código Civil, art. 2341; Estatuto Tributario, art. 26), stating the version-in-force

Route by court: constitutionality and tutela review → Corte Constitucional (corteconstitucional.gov.co, sentencias C-/T-/SU- number/year); ordinary civil, criminal and labour cassation → Corte Suprema de Justicia (cortesuprema.gov.co); administrative and tax litigation → Consejo de Estado (consejodeestado.gov.co)
For tax, the DIAN (dian.gov.co) issues conceptos and resoluciones that bind the administration; contested assessments go to the Consejo de Estado

Route regulatory questions: banking, insurance and securities (integrated regulator) → Superintendencia Financiera (superfinanciera.gov.co); monetary matters → Banco de la República (banrep.gov.co); competition, consumer protection, data protection AND industrial property (trademarks/patents) → Superintendencia de Industria y Comercio (sic.gov.co); company law and insolvency → Superintendencia de Sociedades (supersociedades.gov.co); medicines → INVIMA (invima.gov.co)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) ley/decreto (SUIN/Secretaría Senado), (ii) sentencia (Corte Constitucional/Suprema/Consejo de Estado), (iii) concepto DIAN, (iv) regulator norm — and give the pinpoint (artículo, considerando) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: suin-juriscol.gov.co is the Ministry of Justice consolidated normative system; secretariasenado.gov.co hosts convenient consolidated codes — cross-check the official gazette for authenticity
The SIC (sic.gov.co) is unusual in concentrating competition, consumer, data protection and industrial property — route all four there

Several sites (secretariasenado.gov.co, consejodeestado.gov.co, dian.gov.co, superfinanciera.gov.co) block bare curl but load via browser; corteconstitucional.gov.co apex 404s while its content loads on inner paths
Constitutional Court sentencias are searchable by C-/T-/SU- number on corteconstitucional.gov.co

name: UAE
description: United Arab Emirates, all legal disciplines: federal legislation and the government portal, the federal courts and the common-law financial free zones (DIFC, ADGM), and the authorities across civil, commercial, tax, financial, securities, labour and IP law
instructions: Cite federal legislation by Federal Decree-Law/Law number and year and article from the official legislation portal (uaelegislation.gov.ae); the UAE is a civil-law federation with Sharia influence, and u.ae is the unified government portal

Distinguish the onshore federal system from the two common-law financial free zones: the DIFC (difc.ae, courts at difccourts.ae, regulator DFSA at dfsa.ae) and Abu Dhabi Global Market (adgm.com) apply their own English-language common-law regimes and are separate jurisdictions — always state whether a question is onshore or in a free zone
For tax, the Federal Tax Authority (tax.gov.ae) administers VAT, excise and the federal Corporate Tax (effective 2023) and issues public clarifications and guides binding the authority

Route regulatory questions: banking and monetary matters → Central Bank of the UAE (centralbank.ae); onshore securities and commodities → SCA (sca.gov.ae); DIFC financial services → DFSA (dfsa.ae); economy, companies and IP → Ministry of Economy (economy.gov.ae); labour → MOHRE (mohre.gov.ae)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) federal decree-law/law (uaelegislation.gov.ae) or free-zone regulation (DIFC/ADGM), (ii) court judgment (federal or DIFC/ADGM Courts), (iii) FTA clarification, (iv) regulator rule — and give the pinpoint (article) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: uaelegislation.gov.ae is the official federal legislation portal (Arabic/English); it and centralbank.ae/dfsa.ae/adgm.com block bare curl but load via browser
DIFC and ADGM publish their own laws, regulations and court judgments (difc.ae, difccourts.ae, adgm.com) — treat them as separate common-law jurisdictions
FTA public clarifications and guides are on tax.gov.ae; Corporate Tax guidance is issued there
Emirate-level rules (e.g. Dubai, Abu Dhabi) exist alongside federal law — flag emirate specificity where relevant

name: Saudi Arabia
description: Saudi Arabia, all legal disciplines: the Bureau of Experts laws database and the Umm al-Qura gazette, the Ministry of Justice and Board of Grievances, and the authorities across commercial, tax/zakat, financial, capital-market, competition, data and IP law
instructions: Cite regulations (nizam) by name and article from the Bureau of Experts at the Council of Ministers laws database (laws.boe.gov.sa); Saudi Arabia is a Sharia-based system where royal decrees promulgate regulations published in the Umm al-Qura gazette (uqn.gov.sa)

Route by court: the general and commercial courts under the Ministry of Justice (moj.gov.sa) apply Sharia and codified regulations; administrative disputes go to the Board of Grievances / Diwan al-Mazalim (bog.gov.sa); note the 2023 Civil Transactions Law codified much of contract and tort
For tax and zakat, the Zakat, Tax and Customs Authority (zatca.gov.sa) administers zakat, corporate income tax, VAT and customs and issues guidelines and rulings binding the authority

Route regulatory questions: banking and monetary matters → Saudi Central Bank / SAMA (sama.gov.sa); capital markets → CMA (cma.org.sa); competition → the General Authority for Competition (gac.gov.sa); data and AI, including PDPL → SDAIA (sdaia.gov.sa); intellectual property → SAIP (saip.gov.sa)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) nizam/royal decree (Bureau of Experts/Umm al-Qura), (ii) court/Board of Grievances judgment, (iii) ZATCA guideline, (iv) regulator rule — and give the pinpoint (article) where the wording is decisive; distinguish codified regulation from uncodified Sharia principle
search_notes: laws.boe.gov.sa is the official regulations database (Arabic, with English for many); it and sama.gov.sa block bare curl but load via browser
The Umm al-Qura gazette (uqn.gov.sa) is the publication of record; ZATCA guidelines and the CTL/VAT regulations are on zatca.gov.sa
SDAIA administers the Personal Data Protection Law; SAIP handles patents, trade marks and copyright

name: Cayman Islands
description: Cayman Islands, principal legal disciplines: consolidated legislation, the courts, financial-services regulation, company registry and data protection
instructions: The primary source is legislation.gov.ky (the Laws of the Cayman Islands, consolidated): cite by Law title and year (Companies Act (2023 Revision)); the Cayman Islands are a common-law jurisdiction, so case law of the Grand Court and Court of Appeal is central
Court judgments are on judicial.ky (Judicial Administration — Grand Court, Court of Appeal), with final appeal to the Privy Council; cite by cause number and neutral citation

Route regulatory questions: financial services (banks, funds, insurance, trusts, VASPs) → CIMA (cima.ky), whose rules, statements of guidance and regulatory procedures are the practical core; data protection → the Ombudsman (ombudsman.ky), which supervises the Data Protection Act; company and register matters → the General Registry (ciregistry.ky); general government at gov.ky
There is no direct taxation in the Cayman Islands — for cross-border tax substance and reporting rely on CIMA and the DITC (economic substance, CRS/FATCA) under gov.ky

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Law/Regulations (legislation.gov.ky), (ii) Grand Court/CICA judgment, (iii) CIMA rule or guidance — and give the pinpoint (section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: legislation.gov.ky serves the consolidated Revisions of the Laws; CIMA (cima.ky) publishes its regulatory handbook, rules and statements of guidance
judicial.ky hosts Grand Court and Court of Appeal judgments; financial-services and insolvency case law is significant
Economic-substance and CRS/FATCA guidance sits with the DITC under gov.ky

name: Algeria
description: Algeria, all legal disciplines: the Journal Officiel and consolidated legislation, the Supreme Court and Council of State, Parliament, and the authorities for tax, monetary policy and securities
instructions: Cite legislation by law/ordonnance number and article; the Journal Officiel de la République Algérienne (joradp.dz) is the publication of record and holds consolidated texts (Code civil; Code des impôts) — Algeria is a civil-law system (French-derived) with some Islamic-law elements in personal status
Route by court: civil and criminal cassation → Cour suprême (coursupreme.dz); administrative litigation → Conseil d'État (conseildetat.dz); the Assemblée Populaire Nationale (apn.dz) legislates
For tax, the Direction Générale des Impôts (mfdgi.gov.dz) administers the tax codes and issues circulaires and instructions binding the administration
Route regulatory questions: monetary matters and banking → Banque d'Algérie (bank-of-algeria.dz); securities → COSOB (cosob.org)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) loi/ordonnance (Journal Officiel), (ii) arrêt (Cour suprême/Conseil d'État), (iii) circulaire DGI, (iv) regulator norm — and give the pinpoint (article) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: joradp.dz serves the Journal Officiel as PDFs by year/number (Arabic and French); bank-of-algeria.dz blocks bare curl but loads via browser
Cour suprême and Conseil d'État publish selected decisions on their sites
DGI circulaires and the consolidated tax codes are on mfdgi.gov.dz

name: Armenia
description: Armenia, all legal disciplines: the legal information system (ARLIS), the Constitutional Court and the courts (with the DataLex case-law system), parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition and IP law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text in ARLIS (arlis.am), the Armenian Legal Information System: cite statutes by name and article in the version-in-force (Armenia recodified its Civil, Criminal and Tax Codes in recent years — check the operative code)
Route by court: constitutionality → Constitutional Court (concourt.am); the ordinary and cassation courts are on court.am, with case law in the DataLex system (datalex.am), searchable by case number
For tax, the State Revenue Committee (src.am) administers the Tax Code and issues clarifications binding the administration

Route regulatory questions: banking, insurance, securities and monetary matters (integrated supervisor) → Central Bank of Armenia (cba.am); competition → the Competition Protection Commission (competition.am); intellectual property → the IP Office AIPA (aipa.am); the National Assembly (parliament.am) enacts law

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) law/code (ARLIS), (ii) court decision (DataLex), (iii) SRC clarification, (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (article) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: arlis.am is the official consolidated legal database; datalex.am is the judiciary's case-law system searchable by case number
The Central Bank of Armenia (cba.am) is the single financial regulator; the State Revenue Committee site is src.am
parliament.am and src.am may block bare curl but load via browser; much content is in Armenian with some English
Verify which recodified Code version is in force — Armenia has enacted new Civil, Criminal and Tax Codes in recent years

name: Bahrain
description: Bahrain, principal legal disciplines: consolidated legislation, the Ministry of Justice, and the authorities for tax, financial supervision, data protection and IP
instructions: Cite legislation by law/decree number and article from the Legislation & Legal Opinion Commission (legalaffairs.gov.bh), which holds the consolidated laws and the Official Gazette; Bahrain is a civil-law/Sharia mixed system
The courts sit under the Ministry of Justice (moj.gov.bh), with civil, criminal and Sharia jurisdictions; the Constitutional Court reviews constitutionality
For tax, the National Bureau for Revenue (nbr.gov.bh) administers VAT and excise and issues guidance binding the Bureau

Route regulatory questions: banking, insurance, capital markets and monetary policy (single integrated regulator) → Central Bank of Bahrain (cbb.gov.bh), whose Rulebook is the core of financial regulation; data protection (PDPL) → the Personal Data Protection Authority (pdp.gov.bh); industry, commerce and IP → MOIC (moic.gov.bh)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) law/decree (Legal Affairs/Official Gazette), (ii) court judgment, (iii) NBR guidance, (iv) CBB Rulebook module — and give the pinpoint (article) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: legalaffairs.gov.bh is the consolidated legislation and Official Gazette source (Arabic/English); it and nbr.gov.bh/moic.gov.bh block bare curl but load via browser
The CBB Rulebook (cbb.gov.bh) is a modular rulebook central to Bahrain's role as a Gulf financial centre
PDPL guidance is on pdp.gov.bh

name: Bangladesh
description: Bangladesh, principal legal disciplines: the consolidated Laws of Bangladesh, the Supreme Court and Parliament, and the authorities for tax, securities, monetary policy and IP
instructions: Cite statutes by Act name/number and section from the Laws of Bangladesh on the Legislative and Parliamentary Affairs Division portal (bdlaws.minlaw.gov.bd), the consolidated database; Bangladesh is a common-law jurisdiction

Cite case law by reporter/court: the Supreme Court (supremecourt.gov.bd) comprises the Appellate Division and the High Court Division, which exercises constitutional/writ jurisdiction; Parliament (parliament.gov.bd) legislates
For tax, the National Board of Revenue (nbr.gov.bd) administers income tax, VAT and customs and issues SROs and circulars binding the administration
Route regulatory questions: securities → BSEC (sec.gov.bd); monetary policy and banking → Bangladesh Bank (bb.org.bd); patents, designs and trade marks → DPDT (dpdt.gov.bd)
Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Act/SRO (Laws of Bangladesh), (ii) judgment, (iii) NBR circular, (iv) regulator rule — and give the pinpoint (section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: bdlaws.minlaw.gov.bd is the consolidated Laws of Bangladesh, searchable by Act; it and parliament.gov.bd block bare curl but load via browser
NBR SROs and income-tax/VAT guidance are on nbr.gov.bd
Bangladesh Bank circulars are on bb.org.bd

name: Bermuda
description: Bermuda, principal legal disciplines: consolidated legislation, government and parliament, financial-services regulation and data protection
instructions: The primary source is bermudalaws.bm (the Laws of Bermuda, consolidated): cite by Act title and year; Bermuda is a common-law jurisdiction, so Supreme Court and Court of Appeal case law is central, with final appeal to the Privy Council
Government policy and public services are on gov.bm; the legislature is at parliament.bm; there is no separate constitutional court

Route regulatory questions: financial services (insurance and reinsurance, banking, investment, digital assets) → the Bermuda Monetary Authority (bma.bm), a leading (re)insurance regulator; data protection under PIPA → the Office of the Privacy Commissioner (privacy.bm)
There is no corporate income tax historically, though Bermuda enacted a corporate income tax for large MNE groups (Pillar Two-aligned) effective 2025 — check the current CIT position via gov.bm
Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Act (bermudalaws.bm), (ii) Supreme Court/CA judgment, (iii) BMA rule or guidance — and give the pinpoint (section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: bermudalaws.bm is the official consolidated Laws of Bermuda; the BMA (bma.bm) publishes its insurance and other prudential rules, codes and guidance
gov.bm blocks bare curl but loads via browser; the 2023 Corporate Income Tax Act material is under gov.bm
PrivCom guidance on PIPA is on privacy.bm

name: Bolivia
description: Bolivia, all legal disciplines: the Gaceta Oficial, the Supreme and Plurinational Constitutional Courts, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, banking and IP law
instructions: Cite legislation by law number and article; the official publication is the Gaceta Oficial de Bolivia (gacetaoficialdebolivia.gob.bo), which holds the laws and codes (Código Civil; Código Tributario)
Route by court: constitutionality → Tribunal Constitucional Plurinacional (tcpbolivia.bo, sentencias constitucionales by number/year); ordinary cassation → Tribunal Supremo de Justicia (tsj.bo)
For tax, the Servicio de Impuestos Nacionales (impuestos.gob.bo) administers the Código Tributario and issues resoluciones normativas de directorio binding the administration

Route regulatory questions: monetary matters → Banco Central de Bolivia (bcb.gob.bo); financial-system supervision (banks, securities, insurance) → ASFI (asfi.gob.bo); industrial property → SENAPI (senapi.gob.bo)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) ley/decreto (Gaceta Oficial), (ii) sentencia (TCP/TSJ), (iii) resolución normativa (SIN), (iv) regulator norm — and give the pinpoint (artículo) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: gacetaoficialdebolivia.gob.bo is the authoritative gazette; the TCP (tcpbolivia.bo) publishes sentencias constitucionales searchable by number
ASFI (asfi.gob.bo) is the integrated financial-system regulator; SIN resoluciones are on impuestos.gob.bo
Some sites block bare curl but load via browser

name: British Virgin Islands
description: British Virgin Islands, principal legal disciplines: government and legislation, financial-services regulation, and case law via the Eastern Caribbean Supreme Court
instructions: Primary legislation (the Laws of the Virgin Islands, including the BVI Business Companies Act) is accessed via the Government of the Virgin Islands (bvi.gov.vg) and the Financial Services Commission; the BVI are a common-law jurisdiction

Case law comes from the Eastern Caribbean Supreme Court (eccourts.org), which provides the High Court and Court of Appeal for the BVI, with a specialist Commercial Court; final appeal is to the Privy Council — cite by ECSC claim number and neutral citation

Route regulatory questions: financial services (business companies, funds, investment business, insurance, trusts, VASPs) → the BVI Financial Services Commission (bvifsc.vg), which also maintains the companies and IP registers
There is no direct taxation of BVI companies — economic-substance and beneficial-ownership requirements are administered by the FSC/ITA under bvi.gov.vg
Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Act (BVI legislation), (ii) ECSC judgment, (iii) FSC regulation or guidance — and give the pinpoint (section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: The BVI FSC (bvifsc.vg) is the practical hub for legislation, regulatory codes and the registers; it blocks bare curl but loads via browser
eccourts.org hosts ECSC judgments including the BVI Commercial Court, central to offshore litigation
There is no comprehensive free official BVI legislation portal — reach statutes via bvi.gov.vg and the FSC

name: Bulgaria
description: Bulgaria, all legal disciplines: the State Gazette (Darzhaven vestnik) and consolidated legislation (Lex.bg), the Constitutional Court, the Supreme Court of Cassation and Supreme Administrative Court, parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The authoritative publication is the State Gazette (Darzhaven vestnik) at dv.parliament.bg; for consolidated texts the widely used database is lex.bg — cite statutes by name and article and the DV reference (Закон за задълженията и договорите, чл. 45; ЗКПО, ДВ бр./year)

Route by court: constitutionality → Konstitutsionen sad (constcourt.bg, решение № /year); civil and criminal cassation → Varhoven kasatsionen sad (vks.bg); administrative and tax cassation → Varhoven administrativen sad (sac.justice.bg)
For tax, distinguish binding law from the revenue agency's practice: NAP guidance and stanovishta (nra.bg; Ministry of Finance on minfin.bg) bind the administration, not the courts

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: banking and monetary matters → Balgarska narodna banka (bnb.bg); non-bank financial supervision (capital markets, insurance, pensions) → Komisia za finansov nadzor (fsc.bg); competition → KZK (cpc.bg); data protection → KZLD (cpdp.bg); industrial property → Patentno vedomstvo (bpo.bg); medicines → BDA (bda.bg)
Distinguish binding law from interpretive positions: BNB/FSC naredbi bind supervised entities while their guidance does not; NAP stanovishta are administrative — label each and cite by number

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) zakon/naredba (DV), (ii) reshenie (court), (iii) NAP stanovishte, (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (chlen, motivi) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: The State Gazette is at dv.parliament.bg (search issues by number/year); lex.bg is a reliable private consolidated database indexed by article — cross-check the DV for authenticity
The Supreme Administrative Court's domain is sac.justice.bg (the older sac.government.bg no longer resolves); VKS and the Constitutional Court publish decisions on their own sites by number/year
minfin.bg and bda.bg return 403 to bare curl but load via browser; several sites are Bulgarian-only
NAP guidance and stanovishta are on nra.bg; the FSC register and acts are on fsc.bg

name: Costa Rica
description: Costa Rica, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation and jurisprudence (SINALEVI/PGR) and La Gaceta, the Judiciary (including the Sala Constitucional), the Asamblea Legislativa, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, consumer and data-protection law
instructions: Cite legislation by law number and article from SINALEVI, the Procuraduría's consolidated system (pgrweb.go.cr), which also holds administrative doctrine (dictámenes); the official gazette is La Gaceta at the Imprenta Nacional (imprentanacional.go.cr)

Route by court: constitutionality and amparo → the Sala Constitucional of the Corte Suprema (poder-judicial.go.cr); ordinary civil, criminal and contencioso-administrativo also sit within the Poder Judicial; cite by sala, vote number and year
For tax, the Ministerio de Hacienda / Dirección General de Tributación (hacienda.go.cr) issues resoluciones and criterios binding the administration

Route regulatory questions: monetary matters → Banco Central (bccr.fi.cr); banking supervision → SUGEF (sugef.fi.cr) with SUGEVAL, SUGESE and SUPEN for securities, insurance and pensions; competition → COPROCOM (coprocom.go.cr); consumer → MEIC (meic.go.cr); data protection → PRODHAB (prodhab.go.cr)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) ley/decreto (SINALEVI/La Gaceta), (ii) voto (Sala Constitucional/Poder Judicial), (iii) resolución/criterio (Tributación), (iv) regulator norm — and give the pinpoint (artículo, considerando) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: pgrweb.go.cr (SINALEVI) is the authoritative consolidated legislation and PGR dictámenes; imprentanacional.go.cr serves La Gaceta
Sala Constitucional votes are searchable through the Poder Judicial (poder-judicial.go.cr) — the Nexus/jurisprudencia system
Several .go.cr and .fi.cr sites block bare curl but load via browser; SUGEF, SUGEVAL, SUGESE and SUPEN share the .fi.cr financial-regulator family under the BCCR umbrella

name: Croatia
description: Croatia, all legal disciplines: the official gazette and consolidated legislation (Narodne novine, Zakon.hr), the Constitutional and Supreme Courts, parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The authoritative publication is Narodne novine (narodne-novine.nn.hr), which also serves consolidated pročišćeni tekstovi; the private zakon.hr is a convenient consolidated database — cite statutes by name and article and the NN reference (Zakon o obveznim odnosima, čl. 1045; Zakon o porezu na dobit, NN br./year)

Route by court: constitutionality → Ustavni sud (usud.hr, cited U-.../year); civil, criminal and administrative cassation → Vrhovni sud (vsrh.hr); case law is searchable in the SupraNova/portal sudske prakse
For tax, distinguish binding law from Porezna uprava practice: mišljenja and guidance (porezna-uprava.hr; Ministry of Finance on mfin.gov.hr) state the administration's position

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: banking and monetary matters → Hrvatska narodna banka (hnb.hr); non-bank financial services (capital markets, insurance, pensions) → HANFA (hanfa.hr); competition → AZTN (aztn.hr); data protection → AZOP (azop.hr); industrial property → DZIV (dziv.hr); medicines → HALMED (halmed.hr)

Distinguish binding law from interpretive positions: HNB/HANFA odluke bind supervised entities while their guidance does not; Porezna uprava mišljenja are administrative — label each and cite by class/number

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) zakon/pravilnik (NN), (ii) odluka/presuda (court), (iii) porezno mišljenje, (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (članak, obrazloženje) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: narodne-novine.nn.hr serves each issue (Službeni dio) and consolidated texts (pročišćeni tekstovi) — search by NN number/year; zakon.hr indexes consolidated laws by article
Constitutional Court decisions are at usud.hr; Supreme and lower court practice is via the judiciary's sudska praksa portal (linked from vsrh.hr)
porezna-uprava.hr blocks bare curl but loads via browser; its mišljenja database is searchable by topic
HANFA (hanfa.hr) and HNB (hnb.hr) publish their acts and registers on their own sites

name: Cyprus
description: Cyprus, all legal disciplines: legislation and case law (CyLaw), the Supreme Court, parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection and company law
instructions: Cyprus is a common-law/mixed jurisdiction: the leading free source is CyLaw (cylaw.org) for both legislation (the Laws of Cyprus) and case law — cite statutes by law number and year and judgments by court, parties and date; the House of Representatives (parliament.cy) enacts primary law
Route by court: the Supreme Court (supremecourt.gov.cy) sits at the apex (with the Supreme Constitutional Court and the Court of Appeal after the 2023 reform); cite decisions by case number and year
For tax, the Tax Department under the Ministry of Finance (mof.gov.cy, tax.mof.gov.cy) issues circulars and interpretive guidance binding the administration

Route regulatory questions: securities, investment firms and funds → CySEC (cysec.gov.cy); banking and monetary matters → Central Bank of Cyprus (centralbank.cy); competition → Commission for the Protection of Competition (competition.gov.cy); data protection → Commissioner for Personal Data Protection (dataprotection.gov.cy); company and IP registration → Registrar of Companies and Intellectual Property (companies.gov.cy)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) statute (Laws of Cyprus/CyLaw), (ii) judgment, (iii) Tax Department circular, (iv) regulator guidance — and give the pinpoint (section, paragraph) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: cylaw.org is the practical entry point — free, indexed, with consolidated legislation and full-text Supreme Court decisions (Cyprus is a common-law jurisdiction, so case law matters greatly)
Government sites (supremecourt.gov.cy, competition.gov.cy, dataprotection.gov.cy) resolve on their www host and block bare curl — reach www.<domain> or the specific document
CySEC (cysec.gov.cy) publishes its directives, circulars and decisions in English
The Tax Department circulars are under mof.gov.cy / tax.mof.gov.cy

name: Czech Republic
description: Czech Republic, all legal disciplines: the official consolidated collection of laws (e-Sbírka) and the widely used Zákony pro lidi, the Constitutional, Supreme and Supreme Administrative Courts, both chambers of parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text: the official e-Sbírka (e-sbirka.cz, the electronic Collection of Laws operative since 2024) and, in practice, zakonyprolidi.cz — cite statutes by number/year and section (zákon č. 89/2012 Sb., § 2910; zákon o daních z příjmů č. 586/1992 Sb.)

Route by court: constitutionality → Ústavní soud (usoud.cz, cited by sp. zn. e.g. Pl. ÚS / III. ÚS); civil and criminal → Nejvyšší soud (nsoud.cz); administrative and tax → Nejvyšší správní soud (nssoud.cz) — all publish full-text decisions searchable by spisová značka
For tax, distinguish binding law from the Finanční správa practice: pokyny řady D and guidance (financnisprava.cz; Ministry of Finance policy on mfcr.cz) bind the administration, not the courts

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: banking, insurance, capital markets and monetary policy (integrated supervisor) → Česká národní banka (cnb.cz); competition and public procurement → ÚOHS (uohs.cz); data protection → ÚOOÚ (uoou.cz); industrial property → ÚPV (upv.cz); medicines → SÚKL (sukl.cz)
Distinguish binding law from soft law: ČNB vyhlášky bind while its úřední sdělení are guidance; tax pokyny D are administrative — label each and cite by number

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) zákon/vyhláška (Sbírka), (ii) rozhodnutí (sp. zn.), (iii) pokyn D, (iv) regulator guidance — and give the pinpoint (§, právní věta) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: e-sbirka.cz is the official electronic Collection of Laws (and eSbírka/eLegislativa) providing consolidated texts; zakonyprolidi.cz is a private but reliable consolidated database, well indexed by § — cross-check against e-Sbírka for authenticity
All three apex courts (usoud.cz, nsoud.cz, nssoud.cz) run open decision databases searchable by spisová značka — NALUS for the Constitutional Court
ČNB (cnb.cz) consolidates financial supervision; its regulations and official information are under cnb.cz/en/legislation
Finanční správa pokyny (D-series) and information are on financnisprava.cz

name: Dominican Republic
description: Dominican Republic, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation, the Constitutional Court and the Judiciary, Congress, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, banking, securities, competition and IP law
instructions: Cite legislation by law number and article; the Consultoría Jurídica del Poder Ejecutivo (consultoria.gov.do) publishes laws and decrees, and the Senado (senado.gob.do) carries the legislative record

Route by court: constitutionality → Tribunal Constitucional (tribunalconstitucional.gob.do, sentencias TC/number/year); ordinary civil, criminal and the Suprema Corte de Justicia sit within the Poder Judicial (poderjudicial.gob.do)
For tax, the Dirección General de Impuestos Internos (dgii.gov.do) administers the Código Tributario and issues normas generales binding the administration

Route regulatory questions: monetary matters → Banco Central (bancentral.gov.do); banking → Superintendencia de Bancos (sb.gob.do); securities → Superintendencia del Mercado de Valores (simv.gob.do); competition → PRO-COMPETENCIA (procompetencia.gob.do); industrial property → ONAPI (onapi.gob.do)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) ley/decreto (Consultoría/Congreso), (ii) sentencia (TC/Poder Judicial), (iii) norma general (DGII), (iv) regulator norm — and give the pinpoint (artículo) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: consultoria.gov.do is the practical source for consolidated laws and decrees; the Tribunal Constitucional publishes sentencias by number on tribunalconstitucional.gob.do
The banking supervisor is at sb.gob.do (Superintendencia de Bancos); DGII normas generales are on dgii.gov.do
Some sites block bare curl but load via browser

name: Ecuador
description: Ecuador, all legal disciplines: the Registro Oficial and legislation (Asamblea Nacional), the National Court of Justice and the Constitutional Court, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, banking, companies-and-securities and IP law
instructions: Cite legislation by name and article; the official gazette is the Registro Oficial (registroficial.gob.ec) and the Asamblea Nacional (asambleanacional.gob.ec) carries the legislative record and consolidated codes (Código Civil; Código Orgánico Tributario)

Route by court: constitutionality and selected 'acción extraordinaria de protección' → Corte Constitucional (corteconstitucional.gob.ec); cassation and the ordinary jurisdiction → Corte Nacional de Justicia (cortenacional.gob.ec)
For tax, the Servicio de Rentas Internas (sri.gob.ec) administers the Código Tributario and issues circulares and absolución de consultas binding the administration

Route regulatory questions: monetary matters → Banco Central del Ecuador (bce.fin.ec, note Ecuador is dollarised); banking → Superintendencia de Bancos (superbancos.gob.ec); companies and securities → Superintendencia de Compañías, Valores y Seguros (supercias.gob.ec); intellectual property → SENADI (derechosintelectuales.gob.ec). Competition is supervised by the SCPM, whose site was unreachable at verification — confirm its current domain before relying on it

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) ley/código (Registro Oficial/Asamblea), (ii) sentencia (Corte Constitucional/Nacional), (iii) circular/consulta SRI, (iv) regulator norm — and give the pinpoint (artículo) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: The Registro Oficial is at registroficial.gob.ec (single 'o'); asambleanacional.gob.ec carries consolidated codes and bills
Corte Constitucional and Corte Nacional publish sentencias on their sites; several .gob.ec sites block bare curl but load via browser
SRI absolución de consultas and circulares are on sri.gob.ec

name: Egypt
description: Egypt, all legal disciplines: the constitutional and cassation courts, Parliament, and the authorities across tax, monetary, non-bank financial, competition and commercial law
instructions: Egypt is a civil-law system (French-derived, with Sharia as a principal source per the Constitution); cite laws by number/year and article, published in the Official Gazette (Al-Waqa'i al-Misriyya) — note there is no single comprehensive free consolidated-code portal, so identify the enacting law and amendments

Route by court: constitutionality → Supreme Constitutional Court (sccourt.gov.eg); civil and criminal cassation → Court of Cassation (cc.gov.eg); administrative litigation → the Council of State (Majlis al-Dawla); the House of Representatives (parliament.gov.eg) legislates
For tax, the Egyptian Tax Authority (eta.gov.eg) administers income tax, VAT and other taxes and issues instructions binding the administration

Route regulatory questions: monetary matters and banking → Central Bank of Egypt (cbe.org.eg); non-bank financial services (capital markets, insurance, leasing) → the Financial Regulatory Authority (fra.gov.eg); competition → the Egyptian Competition Authority (eca.org.eg)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) law (Official Gazette), (ii) judgment (SCC/Cassation/Council of State), (iii) ETA instruction, (iv) regulator rule — and give the pinpoint (article) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: The Supreme Constitutional Court (sccourt.gov.eg) and Court of Cassation (cc.gov.eg) publish selected decisions; parliament.gov.eg and fra.gov.eg block bare curl but load via browser
The FRA (fra.gov.eg) regulates all non-bank financial activity; the CBE (cbe.org.eg) handles banking
ETA guidance and the tax laws are on eta.gov.eg

name: Estonia
description: Estonia, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation in the State Gazette (Riigi Teataja), the Supreme Court (which also exercises constitutional review), parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text in Riigi Teataja (riigiteataja.ee), the official State Gazette: cite statutes by short title and section (VÕS § 1043; TuMS) in the version-in-force — it is fully official and free with point-in-time views

Route by court: Estonia has no separate constitutional court — the Riigikohus (riigikohus.ee) is the Supreme Court and its Constitutional Review Chamber, plus civil, criminal and administrative chambers; cite by case number (e.g. 3-...-...)
For tax, the Maksu- ja Tolliamet (emta.ee) issues guidance; the Ministry of Finance (rahandusministeerium.ee) sets policy — guidance binds the administration, not the courts

Route regulatory questions: financial supervision → Finantsinspektsioon (fi.ee); monetary matters → Eesti Pank (eestipank.ee); competition → Konkurentsiamet (konkurentsiamet.ee); data protection → AKI (aki.ee); industrial property → Patendiamet (epa.ee); medicines → Ravimiamet (ravimiamet.ee)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) seadus/määrus (Riigi Teataja), (ii) Riigikohus decision, (iii) administrative guidance, (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (§, punkt) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: riigiteataja.ee is authoritative, consolidated and free, with stable act URLs and point-in-time (kehtiv/redaktsioon) versions and some English translations
Riigikohus decisions are searchable at riigikohus.ee by case number; the Constitutional Review Chamber's case law is the constitutional jurisprudence
rahandusministeerium.ee blocks bare curl but loads via browser
EMTA tax guidance is on emta.ee (English pages available)

name: Georgia
description: Georgia, all legal disciplines: the Legislative Herald (Matsne), the Constitutional and Supreme Courts, parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection and IP law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text in the Legislative Herald of Georgia (matsne.gov.ge): cite statutes by name and article in the version-in-force (Civil Code; Tax Code of Georgia) — matsne.gov.ge is the official publication and consolidation

Route by court: constitutionality → Constitutional Court of Georgia (constcourt.ge); civil, criminal and administrative cassation → Supreme Court of Georgia (supremecourt.ge); cite by case number and date
For tax, the Revenue Service (rs.ge) under the Ministry of Finance administers the Tax Code and issues public and advance rulings

Route regulatory questions: banking, insurance, securities and monetary matters (integrated supervisor) → National Bank of Georgia (nbg.gov.ge); competition → the Competition and Consumer Agency (competition.ge); data protection → the Personal Data Protection Service (pdp.ge); intellectual property → Sakpatenti (sakpatenti.gov.ge); the Parliament (parliament.ge) enacts law

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) law/code (Matsne), (ii) court decision, (iii) Revenue Service ruling, (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (article) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: matsne.gov.ge is the official consolidated Legislative Herald with English translations of major codes; search by document number or title
The Constitutional and Supreme Courts publish decisions on constcourt.ge and supremecourt.ge; several government sites block bare curl but load via browser
The National Bank of Georgia (nbg.gov.ge) is the integrated financial supervisor; the data-protection authority is now the Personal Data Protection Service at pdp.ge
Revenue Service (rs.ge) rulings and the Tax Code are the tax references

name: Greece
description: Greece, all legal disciplines: the Government Gazette (Ethniko Typografeio, FEK), the Council of State and the supreme civil/criminal court (Areios Pagos), the Court of Audit, parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The authoritative publication is the Government Gazette (FEK) at the National Printing House (et.gr): cite laws by number/year and the FEK reference (ν. 4172/2013, ΦΕΚ Α' 167) — Greece lacks a single official consolidated code database, so identify the enacting law and its amendments

Route by court: administrative litigation (including much tax and public law) → Symvoulio tis Epikrateias / Council of State (ste.gr); civil and criminal cassation → Areios Pagos (areiospagos.gr); public-accounts and audit → Court of Audit (elsyn.gr); cite by court, decision number and year
For tax, the administration is the Independent Authority for Public Revenue (AADE, aade.gr): its egkyklioi (circulars) and interpretative documents bind the administration, not the courts

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: capital markets → Hellenic Capital Market Commission (hcmc.gr); banking and monetary matters → Bank of Greece (bankofgreece.gr); competition → Hellenic Competition Commission (epant.gr); data protection → Hellenic DPA (dpa.gr); industrial property → OBI (obi.gr); medicines → EOF (eof.gr)

Distinguish binding law from interpretive positions: AADE circulars, Bank of Greece acts and HCMC decisions are administrative or regulatory acts of varying force — label each by type, number and date

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) nomos/PD (FEK reference), (ii) court decision, (iii) administrative circular, (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (article, skepsi) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: et.gr (National Printing House) serves the FEK as searchable PDFs (search.et.gr) — search by FEK series (Α/Β/…), number and year; there is no free official consolidated-code portal, so cross-check amendments

Most Greek government sites resolve on their www host and block bare curl (ste.gr, elsyn.gr, hcmc.gr, bankofgreece.gr, hellenicparliament.gr among them) — reach www.<domain> or the specific document, or use browser automation
Council of State and Areios Pagos publish decisions on their own sites by number/year; Areios Pagos full text is at areiospagos.gr (Nomologia)
AADE circulars are on aade.gr; older interpretative documents may be found via the FEK where published

name: Guatemala
description: Guatemala, all legal disciplines: legislation (Congreso) and the Diario de Centro América, the Judicial Branch and the Constitutional Court, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, banking and IP law
instructions: Cite legislation by decreto number and article; the Congreso de la República (congreso.gob.gt) holds the laws and the official gazette is the Diario de Centro América (dca.gob.gt)

Route by court: constitutionality and amparo → Corte de Constitucionalidad (cc.gob.gt, expedientes by number/year); ordinary civil, criminal and the Corte Suprema de Justicia → the Organismo Judicial (oj.gob.gt)
For tax, the Superintendencia de Administración Tributaria (sat.gob.gt) administers the tax codes and issues criterios binding the administration

Route regulatory questions: monetary matters → Banco de Guatemala (banguat.gob.gt); banking supervision → Superintendencia de Bancos (sib.gob.gt); industrial property → Registro de la Propiedad Intelectual (rpi.gob.gt)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) decreto/acuerdo (Congreso/DCA), (ii) sentencia (CC/OJ), (iii) criterio SAT, (iv) regulator norm — and give the pinpoint (artículo) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: congreso.gob.gt hosts legislation; the gazette is dca.gob.gt (the older diariodecentroamerica.gob.gt no longer resolves)
Corte de Constitucionalidad expedientes are searchable at cc.gob.gt; several sites (oj.gob.gt, cc.gob.gt, sat.gob.gt) block bare curl but load via browser
SAT and the Superintendencia de Bancos (sib.gob.gt) publish their acuerdos and resoluciones on their sites

name: Guernsey
description: Guernsey (Bailiwick), principal legal disciplines: consolidated legislation, government, financial-services regulation and data protection
instructions: The primary source is Guernsey Legal Resources (guernseylegalresources.gg) for the Laws of Guernsey (Laws, Ordinances and Statutory Instruments): cite by title and year; Guernsey is a common-law-influenced customary-law jurisdiction
Government policy, the States of Guernsey and public services are on gov.gg; the Royal Court sits at the apex of the courts
Route regulatory questions: financial services (banking, insurance, funds, fiduciaries) → GFSC (gfsc.gg), a leading offshore financial regulator; data protection → ODPA (odpa.gg)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Law/Ordinance (Guernsey Legal Resources), (ii) Royal Court judgment, (iii) GFSC rule/guidance — and give the pinpoint (section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: guernseylegalresources.gg is the official legislation site (blocks bare curl; loads via browser); gov.gg carries States business and public services
GFSC (gfsc.gg) publishes its rules, codes and guidance for the finance sector
Guernsey, Alderney and Sark form the Bailiwick — check which island a rule applies to

name: Guyana
description: Guyana, principal legal disciplines: parliament and legislation, tax and monetary authorities, and case law via the Caribbean Court of Justice
instructions: Guyana is a common-law jurisdiction with the Laws of Guyana as its statute base; the Parliament (parliament.gov.gy) enacts and records legislation — cite Acts by chapter/number and year

Final appeals go to the Caribbean Court of Justice (ccj.org), which replaced the Privy Council for Guyana; the CCJ's judgments (and its role as the OECS/CARICOM apex) are the key case-law source, alongside the local High Court and Court of Appeal
For tax, the Guyana Revenue Authority (gra.gov.gy) administers income, corporation and VAT law and issues guidance
Route monetary and banking-supervision questions to the Bank of Guyana (bankofguyana.org.gy)
Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Act (Laws of Guyana), (ii) CCJ/local judgment, (iii) GRA guidance — and give the pinpoint (section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: There is no comprehensive free official consolidated legislation portal — reach statutes via parliament.gov.gy and secondary compilations of the Laws of Guyana
ccj.org hosts Caribbean Court of Justice judgments (final appellate court for Guyana)
GRA (gra.gov.gy) publishes tax guidance and forms

name: Hungary
description: Hungary, all legal disciplines: the National Legislation Database (Nemzeti Jogszabálytár) and the Magyar Közlöny gazette, the Constitutional Court and the Kúria, the courts portal, parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text in the Nemzeti Jogszabálytár (njt.hu): cite statutes by year and number and section (2013. évi V. törvény a Ptk. 6:519. §; 1996. évi LXXXI. törvény a Tao. tv.) in the version-in-force; the promulgated text is in Magyar Közlöny (magyarkozlony.hu)

Route by court: constitutionality → Alkotmánybíróság (alkotmanybirosag.hu, decisions cited e.g. 3/2024. (III. 1.) AB határozat); ordinary and administrative cassation and uniformity decisions → Kúria (kuria-birosag.hu); the courts portal birosag.hu carries case law (BHGY collection)
For tax, distinguish binding law from the tax administration's practice: NAV guidance, tájékoztatók and binding rulings (nav.gov.hu) state the administration's position

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: banking, insurance, capital markets and monetary policy (integrated supervisor since 2013) → Magyar Nemzeti Bank (mnb.hu); competition → GVH (gvh.hu); data protection → NAIH (naih.hu); industrial property → SZTNH (sztnh.gov.hu); medicines → OGYÉI (ogyei.gov.hu)
Distinguish binding law from soft law: MNB rendeletek bind while its ajánlások are recommendations; NAV tájékoztatók are guidance — label each and cite by number

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) törvény/rendelet (NJT/Magyar Közlöny), (ii) bírósági határozat, (iii) NAV guidance, (iv) MNB soft law — and give the pinpoint (§, indokolás) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: njt.hu is the authoritative consolidated database (Nemzeti Jogszabálytár) — search by year/number; it blocks the sandbox's automated fetch but loads in a browser, so open the specific act or use browser automation
Magyar Közlöny (magyarkozlony.hu) publishes each issue as PDF for the promulgated text
Constitutional Court decisions are searchable at alkotmanybirosag.hu; the Kúria and lower courts' precedents are in the BHGY (Bírósági Határozatok Gyűjteménye) via birosag.hu
MNB regulations and recommendations are at mnb.hu/felugyelet/szabalyozas

name: Iceland
description: Iceland, all legal disciplines: consolidated law and the gazette (Alþingi, Stjórnartíðindi), the Supreme Court and the courts, parliament and government, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated law collection (lagasafn) on althingi.is: cite statutes by number/year and article (lög nr. 90/2003 um tekjuskatt; almenn hegningarlög nr. 19/1940) in the version-in-force; the official gazette is Stjórnartíðindi (stjornartidindi.is)
Route by court: the Hæstiréttur (haestirettur.is) is the Supreme Court, above the Landsréttur (Court of Appeal) and district courts on domstolar.is; cite by case number
For tax, Skatturinn (Iceland Revenue and Customs, skatturinn.is) issues binding advance rulings and guidance; the government (stjornarradid.is) sets policy

Route regulatory questions: financial supervision and monetary matters (the FME merged into the central bank in 2020) → Seðlabanki Íslands (sedlabanki.is); competition → Samkeppniseftirlitið (samkeppni.is); data protection → Persónuvernd (personuvernd.is); industrial property → Hugverkastofan (hugverk.is); medicines → Lyfjastofnun (lyfjastofnun.is)
Iceland is in the EEA: identify where a rule implements EEA-incorporated EU law, and note EFTA Court case law can bear on interpretation
Always classify what supports a claim — (i) lög/reglugerð, (ii) court decision, (iii) tax ruling, (iv) regulator guidance — and give the pinpoint (grein) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: althingi.is holds the lagasafn (consolidated statutes) and the legislative process; stjornartidindi.is is the official gazette of record
Hæstiréttur (haestirettur.is) and the courts (domstolar.is) publish decisions by case number; althingi.is blocks bare curl but loads via browser
The Central Bank of Iceland (sedlabanki.is) exercises financial supervision after the 2020 FME merger
Skatturinn advance rulings (bindandi álit) are on skatturinn.is

name: Indonesia
description: Indonesia, all legal disciplines: the national legal-documentation database, the Constitutional and Supreme Courts, the DPR, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, monetary, competition, IP and drug law
instructions: Cite legislation by type/number/year and article from peraturan.go.id (the national legal-documentation database, JDIH), e.g. UU No. 40 Tahun 2007 (Perseroan Terbatas); distinguish laws (UU), government regulations (PP) and ministerial/agency regulations

Route by court: constitutional review → Mahkamah Konstitusi (mkri.id, putusan by number/year); cassation and judicial review → Mahkamah Agung (mahkamahagung.go.id), whose decisions are in its putusan database; the DPR (dpr.go.id) legislates
For tax, the Directorate General of Taxes (pajak.go.id) issues surat edaran and regulations interpreting the tax laws binding the administration

Route regulatory questions: banking, insurance, capital markets (integrated regulator) → OJK (ojk.go.id); monetary policy → Bank Indonesia (bi.go.id); competition → KPPU (kppu.go.id); intellectual property → DJKI (dgip.go.id); food and drugs → BPOM (pom.go.id); the data-protection authority under the 2022 PDP Law is being established, with the digital ministry Komdigi (komdigi.go.id, formerly Kominfo) currently central — verify the operative authority

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) UU/PP/regulation (peraturan.go.id), (ii) putusan (MK/MA), (iii) DGT surat edaran, (iv) regulator regulation — and give the pinpoint (pasal) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: peraturan.go.id is the national JDIH consolidated database; most Indonesian gov sites (peraturan.go.id, mkri.id, mahkamahagung.go.id, dpr.go.id, ojk.go.id) block bare curl but load via browser
Mahkamah Agung decisions are in putusan3.mahkamahagung.go.id (covered by mahkamahagung.go.id scoping)
The communications ministry rebranded from Kominfo to Komdigi (komdigi.go.id) in 2024
OJK is the integrated financial-services regulator (banking supervision moved from BI)

name: Israel
description: Israel, all legal disciplines: Knesset legislation, the Supreme Court and case law, and the authorities across civil, commercial, tax, securities, monetary, competition, privacy and IP law
instructions: Cite statutes (Basic Laws and ordinary laws) by name and section from Knesset legislation (knesset.gov.il); Israel is a mixed common-law/civil-law system with a strong role for Supreme Court precedent and the Basic Laws as constitutional framework

Cite case law by court and docket: the Supreme Court (supreme.court.gov.il), sitting also as the High Court of Justice (Bagatz), is the apex — cite HCJ/CA numbers; Nevo (nevo.co.il) is the widely used (subscription) case-law database
For tax, the Israel Tax Authority (under gov.il) issues rulings (hachlatot mikda) and circulars (chozrim) binding the authority

Route regulatory questions: securities and markets → ISA (isa.gov.il); monetary policy and banking supervision → Bank of Israel (boi.org.il); competition, privacy (PPA), companies and IP (patents) are administered by ministries/authorities under the gov.il portal and the Ministry of Justice (justice.gov.il)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) statute/Basic Law (Knesset), (ii) judgment (Supreme Court/HCJ), (iii) Tax Authority ruling/circular, (iv) regulator rule — and give the pinpoint (section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: knesset.gov.il hosts legislation; the Reshumot official gazette is the publication of record; supreme.court.gov.il, gov.il and isa.gov.il block the sandbox's automated fetch but load via browser
Nevo (nevo.co.il) is the practical case-law database (subscription); the Supreme Court also publishes decisions on its own site
Tax Authority rulings and circulars, the Companies Registrar and the Patent Office all sit under gov.il

name: Jersey
description: Jersey, principal legal disciplines: consolidated legislation and case law (Jersey Law), government, financial-services regulation and data protection
instructions: The primary source is Jersey Law (jerseylaw.je) for the Revised Laws of Jersey and the Jersey Law Reports: cite statutes by title and year and judgments by JLR/JRC reference — Jersey is a customary-law jurisdiction with strong common-law influence
Government policy, the States Assembly and public services are on gov.je; the Royal Court sits at the apex, with appeals to the Court of Appeal and the Privy Council

Route regulatory questions: financial services (banking, funds, trust company business, insurance) → Jersey Financial Services Commission (jerseyfsc.org), a leading offshore regulator; data protection → Jersey Office of the Information Commissioner (jerseyoic.org)
Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Law/Regulations (Jersey Law), (ii) Royal Court judgment (JLR), (iii) JFSC code/guidance — and give the pinpoint (Article) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: jerseylaw.je is the official free source for the Revised Laws and the Jersey Law Reports, indexed and searchable
JFSC (jerseyfsc.org) publishes the Codes of Practice and guidance notes central to Jersey financial regulation
gov.je carries States Assembly business, budgets and public administration

name: Kuwait
description: Kuwait, principal legal disciplines: the Ministry of Justice, the National Assembly, and the authorities for public finance, monetary policy and capital markets
instructions: Kuwait is a civil-law/Sharia mixed system; cite laws by number/year and article as published in the Official Gazette (Kuwait Al-Yawm) — legislation and legal information are accessed via the Ministry of Justice (moj.gov.kw) and the KILAW/legal databases
The National Assembly / Majlis al-Umma (kna.kw) legislates; the courts sit under the Ministry of Justice, with the Constitutional Court reviewing constitutionality
For public finance and corporate matters, the Ministry of Finance (mof.gov.kw) is the policy authority; Kuwait has no personal income tax and limited corporate tax on foreign entities
Route regulatory questions: monetary matters and banking → Central Bank of Kuwait (cbk.gov.kw); capital markets → the Capital Markets Authority (cma.gov.kw)
Always classify what supports a claim — (i) law (Official Gazette), (ii) court judgment, (iii) ministry/authority guidance — and give the pinpoint (article) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: There is no comprehensive free official consolidated-code portal — reach legislation via moj.gov.kw and the National Assembly (kna.kw); mof.gov.kw blocks bare curl but loads via browser
The CMA (cma.gov.kw) publishes the capital-markets law and executive bylaws
Central Bank instructions to banks are on cbk.gov.kw

name: Latvia
description: Latvia, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation (Likumi.lv) and the official gazette (Latvijas Vēstnesis), the Constitutional and Supreme Courts, parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text on Likumi.lv: cite statutes by title and section (Civillikums, 1635. pants; Uzņēmumu ienākuma nodokļa likums); the official publication is Latvijas Vēstnesis (vestnesis.lv)
Route by court: constitutionality → Satversmes tiesa (satv.tiesa.gov.lv, cited by case number); civil, criminal and administrative cassation → Augstākā tiesa / Senāts (at.gov.lv)
