For tax, the Valsts ieņēmumu dienests (vid.gov.lv) issues methodological guidance and binding advance rulings; the Ministry of Finance (fm.gov.lv) sets policy

Route regulatory questions: banking, insurance, capital markets and monetary matters (integrated in the central bank since 2023) → Latvijas Banka (bank.lv); competition → Konkurences padome (kp.gov.lv); data protection → DVI (dvi.gov.lv); industrial property → Patentu valde (lrpv.gov.lv); medicines → Zāļu valsts aģentūra (zva.gov.lv)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) likums/noteikumi (Likumi.lv), (ii) court decision, (iii) VID guidance, (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (pants, daļa) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: Likumi.lv provides consolidated 'spēkā esošā redakcija' texts with stable IDs; vestnesis.lv is the official gazette of record (the two are run by the same publisher)
Satversmes tiesa (satv.tiesa.gov.lv) and the Senāts (at.gov.lv) publish decisions searchable by case number; Latvijas Banka absorbed the former FKTK's financial-supervision function in 2023
VID methodological materials are on vid.gov.lv
Most Latvian sites answer a plain fetch; use the browser for JS-driven case-law search forms

name: Liechtenstein
description: Liechtenstein, principal legal disciplines: consolidated law (LR) and court decisions, the State Court, parliament and government, and the authorities for financial supervision, tax and data protection
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text on gesetze.li (Landesgesetzblatt / Systematische Rechtssammlung LR): cite statutes by LR number and article (LR 210.0 PGR; SteG) in the version-in-force
Court decisions are published on gerichtsentscheide.li, including the Staatsgerichtshof (constitutional) and the ordinary and administrative courts — cite by case reference and date
For tax and administration, the Steuerverwaltung and other offices sit under the Landesverwaltung (llv.li); the government (regierung.li) and Landtag (landtag.li) make policy and law

Route regulatory questions: financial supervision (banks, funds, insurance, TCSPs) → FMA Liechtenstein (fma-li.li); data protection → Datenschutzstelle (datenschutzstelle.li); Liechtenstein is an EEA member, so identify EEA-implemented EU law
Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Gesetz/Verordnung (LR), (ii) court decision, (iii) FMA guidance — and give the pinpoint (Artikel) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: gesetze.li is the official consolidated Systematische Rechtssammlung with stable LR numbers; gerichtsentscheide.li is the official case-law database
The FMA (fma-li.li) is the single financial-services regulator and publishes guidelines and communications in German and English
llv.li (Landesverwaltung) is the umbrella for the administrative offices, including tax
Most .li sites answer a plain fetch; llv.li may block bare curl

name: Lithuania
description: Lithuania, all legal disciplines: the Register of Legal Acts (TAR) and the Seimas legislation database, the Constitutional Court and the Supreme and Supreme Administrative Courts, parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text in the Teisės aktų registras (e-tar.lt) and the Seimas database (e-seimas.lrs.lt): cite statutes by name and article (Civilinis kodeksas, 6.263 str.; Pelno mokesčio įstatymas) in the version-in-force

Route by court: constitutionality → Konstitucinis Teismas (lrkt.lt, nutarimai by date); civil and criminal cassation → Lietuvos Aukščiausiasis Teismas (lat.lt); administrative → Lietuvos vyriausiasis administracinis teismas (lvat.lt)
For tax, the Valstybinė mokesčių inspekcija (vmi.lt) issues commentaries on the tax laws and binding rulings; these bind the administration

Route regulatory questions: banking, insurance, capital markets and monetary matters (integrated supervisor) → Lietuvos bankas (lb.lt); competition → Konkurencijos taryba (kt.gov.lt); data protection → VDAI (vdai.lrv.lt); industrial property → Valstybinis patentų biuras (vpb.lrv.lt); medicines → VVKT (vvkt.lt)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) įstatymas/nutarimas (TAR), (ii) court decision, (iii) VMI commentary, (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (straipsnis, dalis) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: e-tar.lt and e-seimas.lrs.lt hold consolidated acts (aktuali redakcija) with stable identifiers; most Lithuanian official sites (e-tar.lt, lrkt.lt, lat.lt, lvat.lt, lrs.lt, lb.lt) return 403 to bare curl but load via browser — reach the specific act/decision or use browser automation
The three apex courts publish decisions searchable by case number on their own sites
VMI tax-law commentaries are on vmi.lt
Lietuvos bankas is the single financial supervisor

name: Macau
description: Macau SAR, principal legal disciplines: the Boletim Oficial and consolidated law, the courts and the Legislative Assembly, and the authorities for tax, monetary/financial supervision, data protection and economic affairs
instructions: Cite legislation by law/decree number and article; the Boletim Oficial (bo.io.gov.mo) is the publication of record and holds consolidated legislation — Macau is a civil-law (Portuguese-derived) system under 'one country, two systems'
The courts (court.gov.mo) comprise the Court of First Instance, the Court of Second Instance and the Court of Final Appeal; the Legislative Assembly (al.gov.mo) legislates
For tax, the Financial Services Bureau (DSF, dsf.gov.mo) administers the complementary income tax and other taxes and issues guidance

Route regulatory questions: monetary and financial supervision → AMCM / Monetary Authority of Macao (amcm.gov.mo); data protection → GPDP (gpdp.gov.mo); economic affairs, IP and commerce → the Economic and Technological Development Bureau (economia.gov.mo)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) lei/regulamento (Boletim Oficial), (ii) court decision, (iii) DSF guidance, (iv) regulator norm — and give the pinpoint (artigo) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: The Boletim Oficial is at bo.io.gov.mo (Imprensa Oficial); it publishes in Chinese and Portuguese, both authentic
Most Macau gov sites block bare curl but load via browser; the courts publish decisions on court.gov.mo
AMCM (amcm.gov.mo) is the monetary and financial regulator

name: Malaysia
description: Malaysia (federal), all legal disciplines: the Laws of Malaysia (AGC) and the courts, Parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, securities, monetary, competition, data protection, IP and company law
instructions: Cite statutes by Act number/short title and section from the Laws of Malaysia on the Attorney-General's Chambers portal (lom.agc.gov.my), e.g. Companies Act 2016 (Act 777) s.213; Income Tax Act 1967 (Act 53); Malaysia is a common-law jurisdiction

Cite case law by neutral citation or reporter (e.g. [2024] MyFC ; [2023] MLJ) — the Judiciary (kehakiman.gov.my) hosts the Federal Court, Court of Appeal and High Courts; note the parallel Syariah courts for Islamic personal law

For tax, the Inland Revenue Board (hasil.gov.my) issues Public Rulings and guidelines (income tax) and the Royal Malaysian Customs (customs.gov.my) administers indirect taxes — Public Rulings state the Board's interpretation

Route regulatory questions: securities and capital markets → Securities Commission (sc.com.my); banking, insurance and monetary policy → Bank Negara Malaysia (bnm.gov.my); competition → MyCC (mycc.gov.my); data protection (PDPA) → the Personal Data Protection Department (pdp.gov.my); patents, trade marks and designs → MyIPO (myipo.gov.my); companies → SSM (ssm.com.my)
Malaysia is a federation with a dual civil/Syariah system: this source is the federal level; flag Syariah and East-Malaysia (Sabah/Sarawak) specificities where relevant

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Act/subsidiary legislation (Laws of Malaysia), (ii) judgment, (iii) IRB Public Ruling, (iv) regulator guideline — and give the pinpoint (section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: lom.agc.gov.my hosts the consolidated Laws of Malaysia (PDF); many Malaysian gov sites (kehakiman.gov.my, hasil.gov.my, bnm.gov.my, myipo.gov.my) block bare curl (403/406) but load via browser
IRB Public Rulings are on hasil.gov.my and index by number/year
Bank Negara policy documents and the Financial Sector regulatory framework are on bnm.gov.my

name: Malta
description: Malta, all legal disciplines: the consolidated Laws of Malta, the judiciary, parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, consumer, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text on legislation.mt (Laws of Malta): cite by Chapter number and article (Cap. 386 Companies Act, art. 69; Cap. 123 Income Tax Act) in the version-in-force — Malta is a mixed civil/common-law jurisdiction
Route by court: the courts and their judgments are on judiciary.mt (Sentenzi Online), with the Constitutional Court and Court of Appeal at the apex; cite by court, case reference and date
For tax, the Commissioner for Tax and Customs (cfr.gov.mt) issues guidelines and administers the Income Tax Acts; guidance binds the administration

Route regulatory questions: financial services (banking, insurance, investment, funds) under the single regulator → MFSA (mfsa.mt); monetary matters → Central Bank of Malta (centralbankmalta.org); competition and consumer affairs → MCCAA (mccaa.org.mt); data protection → IDPC (idpc.org.mt); industrial property → the Commerce Department (commerce.gov.mt); medicines → Medicines Authority (medicinesauthority.gov.mt)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Chapter/subsidiary legislation (legislation.mt), (ii) judgment (judiciary.mt), (iii) tax guideline, (iv) regulator rulebook/guidance — and give the pinpoint (article, sub-article) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: legislation.mt serves the consolidated Laws of Malta by Chapter with point-in-time versions; judiciary.mt hosts Sentenzi Online (judgments), many in Maltese
Government sites (judiciary.mt, parlament.mt, cfr.gov.mt, mfsa.mt, commerce.gov.mt) block bare curl but load via browser
MFSA is the single financial-services regulator; its rulebook and circulars are on mfsa.mt
The Medicines Authority and MCCAA publish in English

name: Mauritius
description: Mauritius, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation and the Supreme Court, the National Assembly, and the authorities across tax, monetary, financial-services (global business), data-protection and commercial law
instructions: Cite statutes by short title and section from the Laws of Mauritius (accessible via the Supreme Court and the government portal govmu.org); Mauritius is a hybrid civil-law/common-law jurisdiction (French-derived codes with English-style procedure), with final appeal to the Privy Council
Cite case law by the Supreme Court (supremecourt.govmu.org); the National Assembly (mauritiusassembly.govmu.org) legislates

For tax, the Mauritius Revenue Authority (mra.mu) administers income tax, VAT and issues Statements of Practice and rulings binding the authority; Mauritius is a treaty-network and global-business hub, so watch substance and treaty-benefit conditions

Route regulatory questions: monetary matters and banking → Bank of Mauritius (bom.mu); non-bank financial services and global business (the offshore/GBC regime) → the Financial Services Commission (fscmauritius.org); data protection → the Data Protection Office (dataprotection.govmu.org)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Act (Laws of Mauritius), (ii) judgment (Supreme Court/Privy Council), (iii) MRA Statement of Practice/ruling, (iv) FSC rule — and give the pinpoint (section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: The Laws of Mauritius and Supreme Court judgments are via supremecourt.govmu.org and the govmu.org portal; supremecourt.govmu.org and mra.mu block bare curl but load via browser
The FSC (fscmauritius.org) regulates the global-business/GBC sector central to Mauritius's role as an investment platform
MRA Statements of Practice and rulings are on mra.mu

name: Monaco
description: Monaco, principal legal disciplines: consolidated legislation and the Journal de Monaco, the government and the National Council, and data-protection supervision
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text on LegiMonaco (legimonaco.mc): cite laws and ordonnances souveraines by number and date; the official gazette is the Journal de Monaco (journaldemonaco.gouv.mc)
The Prince, the Government (gouv.mc) and the Conseil National (conseil-national.mc) exercise legislative and executive functions; the Tribunal Suprême handles constitutional and administrative review
For data protection, the CCIN (ccin.mc) is the supervisory authority; Monaco is not an EU member but has adequacy-oriented data-protection law

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) loi/ordonnance souveraine (LegiMonaco/Journal de Monaco), (ii) court decision, (iii) administrative guidance — and give the pinpoint (article) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: legimonaco.mc provides consolidated legislation searchable by type/number/date; the Journal de Monaco (journaldemonaco.gouv.mc) is the gazette of record
gouv.mc and journaldemonaco.gouv.mc block bare curl but load via browser
Monaco is a small civil-law jurisdiction; much administrative detail is on gouv.mc

name: New Zealand
description: New Zealand, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation and case law (New Zealand Legislation, Courts of NZ, NZLII), Parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, monetary, competition, privacy, IP and medicines law
instructions: Cite statutes by short title, year and section from New Zealand Legislation (legislation.govt.nz), e.g. Companies Act 1993 s.131; Income Tax Act 2007, using the current reprint; New Zealand is a common-law jurisdiction
Cite case law by neutral citation: Supreme Court as [2024] NZSC 10; Court of Appeal [2023] NZCA; High Court [2023] NZHC — judgments are on courtsofnz.govt.nz and NZLII (nzlii.org)
For tax, Inland Revenue (ird.govt.nz) issues binding rulings, interpretation statements and Standard Practice Statements; distinguish a binding ruling from an operational statement

Route regulatory questions: financial markets conduct → FMA (fma.govt.nz); prudential regulation and monetary policy → Reserve Bank (rbnz.govt.nz); competition and consumer → Commerce Commission (comcom.govt.nz); privacy → Privacy Commissioner (privacy.org.nz); patents, trade marks and designs → IPONZ (iponz.govt.nz); medicines → Medsafe (medsafe.govt.nz)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Act/regulation (NZ Legislation), (ii) judgment (neutral citation), (iii) IRD ruling/statement, (iv) regulator guidance — and give the pinpoint (section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: legislation.govt.nz is the official consolidated source with point-in-time versions; NZLII (nzlii.org) hosts case law and blocks bare curl but loads via browser
courtsofnz.govt.nz publishes senior-court judgments; parliament.nz carries Bills and select-committee material
IRD binding rulings and interpretation statements are on ird.govt.nz (Tax Technical)
rbnz.govt.nz and nzlii.org return 403 to bare curl but load via browser

name: Nicaragua
description: Nicaragua, principal legal disciplines: the legislative digest and gazette, the Judiciary, and the authorities for tax, monetary policy and financial supervision
instructions: Cite legislation by law number and article from the Asamblea Nacional's Digesto Jurídico (legislacion.asamblea.gob.ni), the consolidated legislation system; La Gaceta (Diario Oficial) is the authoritative publication, indexed there
The Corte Suprema de Justicia within the Poder Judicial (poderjudicial.gob.ni) is the apex court; the Asamblea Nacional (asamblea.gob.ni) enacts law
For tax, the Dirección General de Ingresos (dgi.gob.ni) administers the tax code and issues disposiciones binding the administration
Route regulatory questions: monetary matters → Banco Central de Nicaragua (bcn.gob.ni); banking and financial supervision → SIBOIF (siboif.gob.ni)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) ley/decreto (Digesto/La Gaceta), (ii) sentencia (Corte Suprema), (iii) disposición DGI, (iv) regulator norm — and give the pinpoint (artículo) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: legislacion.asamblea.gob.ni (Digesto Jurídico Nicaragüense) is the consolidated legislation source; most Nicaraguan gov sites block bare curl but load via browser
SIBOIF (siboif.gob.ni) supervises banks, insurance and securities; DGI disposiciones técnicas are on dgi.gob.ni
Poder Judicial jurisprudence is on poderjudicial.gob.ni

name: Nigeria
description: Nigeria (federal), all legal disciplines: the Laws of the Federation and legislation, the Supreme Court and case law (NigeriaLII), the National Assembly, and the authorities across tax, monetary, securities, competition, data-protection, corporate and IP law
instructions: Cite statutes by Act name and section from the Laws of the Federation (the PLAC LawsofNigeria database, placng.org) and the National Assembly (nass.gov.ng); Nigeria is a common-law federation with Sharia and customary law in some states for personal matters
Cite case law by reporter/neutral citation: the Supreme Court (supremecourt.gov.ng) is the apex; NigeriaLII (nigerialii.org) is the free case-law database

For tax, the Federal Inland Revenue Service (firs.gov.ng) administers companies income tax, VAT and other federal taxes and issues information circulars binding the Service; note the 2025 tax-reform Acts

Route regulatory questions: monetary matters and banking → Central Bank of Nigeria (cbn.gov.ng); securities and capital markets → SEC (sec.gov.ng); competition and consumer → FCCPC (fccpc.gov.ng); data protection → the Nigeria Data Protection Commission (ndpc.gov.ng); companies → Corporate Affairs Commission (cac.gov.ng)
Nigeria is a federation: this source is the federal level. When a question turns on state law (state taxes, land under the Land Use Act administration, Sharia penal codes in northern states) flag it

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Act (Laws of the Federation), (ii) judgment (Supreme Court/neutral citation), (iii) FIRS circular, (iv) regulator rule — and give the pinpoint (section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: placng.org (PLAC) hosts a practical free Laws of Nigeria database; NigeriaLII (nigerialii.org) is the free case-law source
firs.gov.ng and cac.gov.ng block bare curl but load via browser; FIRS circulars and the 2025 tax-reform materials are on firs.gov.ng
SEC rules and the CBN's guidelines/circulars are on sec.gov.ng and cbn.gov.ng

name: Oman
description: Oman, principal legal disciplines: the Official Gazette (Ministry of Justice and Legal Affairs), and the authorities for tax, monetary policy and financial-services supervision
instructions: Oman is a civil-law/Sharia mixed system; cite Royal Decrees and laws by number/year and article as published in the Official Gazette by the Ministry of Justice and Legal Affairs (mjla.gov.om), which is the consolidated source
The courts (with civil, commercial, criminal and Sharia jurisdictions) and the Administrative Court sit within the Omani judiciary; the Council of Oman legislates jointly with the Sultan
For tax, the Tax Authority (taxoman.gov.om) administers corporate income tax, VAT and withholding tax and issues guidance binding the authority

Route regulatory questions: monetary matters and banking → Central Bank of Oman (cbo.gov.om); capital markets and insurance → the Financial Services Authority (fsa.gov.om, which succeeded the Capital Market Authority in 2024)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Royal Decree/law (Official Gazette), (ii) court judgment, (iii) Tax Authority guidance, (iv) FSA/CBO rule — and give the pinpoint (article) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: mjla.gov.om is the Ministry of Justice and Legal Affairs portal for the Official Gazette and legislation (Arabic, some English)
The former Capital Market Authority is now the Financial Services Authority (fsa.gov.om); its site and cma.gov.om resolve to the same host
Tax Authority guidance is on taxoman.gov.om (the taxpayer system is at tms.taxoman.gov.om)

name: Panama
description: Panama, all legal disciplines: the Gaceta Oficial and legislation (Asamblea Nacional), the Judicial Branch, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, banking, securities, competition, consumer, data protection and IP law
instructions: Cite legislation by law number and article; the official gazette is the Gaceta Oficial (gacetaoficial.gob.pa) and consolidated texts and bills are on the Asamblea Nacional site (asamblea.gob.pa, LegisPan)
The Órgano Judicial (organojudicial.gob.pa) houses the Corte Suprema de Justicia, which also exercises constitutional review (acciones de inconstitucionalidad); cite by sala and date
For tax, the Dirección General de Ingresos (dgi.mef.gob.pa) administers the Código Fiscal and issues resoluciones; Panama's territorial system taxes Panama-source income

Route regulatory questions: banking → Superintendencia de Bancos (superbancos.gob.pa); securities → Superintendencia del Mercado de Valores (supervalores.gob.pa); competition and consumer → ACODECO (acodeco.gob.pa); data protection and transparency → ANTAI (antai.gob.pa); industrial property → DIGERPI under MICI (mici.gob.pa)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) ley/decreto (Gaceta Oficial), (ii) fallo (Corte Suprema), (iii) resolución DGI, (iv) regulator norm — and give the pinpoint (artículo) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: gacetaoficial.gob.pa serves the gazette; asamblea.gob.pa hosts LegisPan (consolidated legislation) and blocks bare curl but loads via browser
The Corte Suprema (organojudicial.gob.pa) publishes fallos including constitutional decisions
Panama is a territorial-tax and international-finance jurisdiction — banking and securities supervision are split between superbancos.gob.pa and supervalores.gob.pa

name: Paraguay
description: Paraguay, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation (BACN) and the Gaceta Oficial, the Judiciary (Corte Suprema), Congress, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, banking and IP law
instructions: Cite legislation by law number and article from the Biblioteca y Archivo Central del Congreso (bacn.gov.py), which holds consolidated legislation; the official gazette is at gacetaoficial.gov.py
The Corte Suprema de Justicia within the Poder Judicial (pj.gov.py) is the apex court and also rules on constitutionality (acción de inconstitucionalidad); the Congreso (congreso.gov.py) enacts law
For tax, the Dirección Nacional de Ingresos Tributarios (dnit.gov.py), which succeeded the SET, administers the tax laws and issues resoluciones binding the administration
Route regulatory questions: monetary and banking-supervision matters → Banco Central del Paraguay (bcp.gov.py); intellectual property → DINAPI (dinapi.gov.py)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) ley/decreto (BACN/Gaceta Oficial), (ii) acuerdo y sentencia (Corte Suprema), (iii) resolución DNIT, (iv) regulator norm — and give the pinpoint (artículo) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: bacn.gov.py is the consolidated legislation database; gacetaoficial.gov.py serves the gazette
The tax authority migrated from the SET to the DNIT (dnit.gov.py); it and congreso.gov.py/bcp.gov.py block bare curl but load via browser
Corte Suprema jurisprudence is on pj.gov.py

name: Peru
description: Peru, all legal disciplines: the consolidated legal system (SPIJ) and El Peruano gazette, the Constitutional Court and the Judiciary, Congress, and the authorities across constitutional, civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, consumer, IP and health law
instructions: Cite legislation by norm type/number and article from the Sistema Peruano de Información Jurídica (spij.minjus.gob.pe), which holds consolidated texts (Código Civil, art. 1969; TUO de la Ley del Impuesto a la Renta); the official gazette is El Peruano (busquedas.elperuano.pe)

Route by court: constitutionality and amparo/habeas → Tribunal Constitucional (tc.gob.pe, sentencias by expediente number); ordinary civil, criminal and the Corte Suprema → the Poder Judicial (pj.gob.pe)
For tax, the SUNAT (sunat.gob.pe) issues informes and resoluciones that bind the administration; the Tribunal Fiscal (under mef.gob.pe) resolves appeals before the judiciary

Route regulatory questions: securities → SMV (smv.gob.pe); banking, insurance and private pensions → SBS (sbs.gob.pe); monetary matters → BCRP (bcrp.gob.pe); competition, consumer protection AND intellectual property → INDECOPI (indecopi.gob.pe); data protection and medicines (DIGEMID) sit under the unified gob.pe portal, routed by path

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) ley/decreto (SPIJ/El Peruano), (ii) sentencia (TC/Poder Judicial), (iii) informe SUNAT/resolución Tribunal Fiscal, (iv) regulator norm — and give the pinpoint (artículo, fundamento) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: spij.minjus.gob.pe is the official consolidated system (some content behind login); busquedas.elperuano.pe serves the gazette and is freely searchable by date
INDECOPI (indecopi.gob.pe) concentrates competition, consumer protection and industrial property — route all three there
Most Peruvian gov sites resolve on www and block bare curl (sunat.gob.pe, sbs.gob.pe, bcrp.gob.pe, pj.gob.pe, congreso.gob.pe) — use the browser or the specific document URL
Data protection and DIGEMID (medicines) are under gob.pe/<entidad>

name: Philippines
description: Philippines, all legal disciplines: the Official Gazette and legislation (LawPhil), the Supreme Court and Congress, and the authorities across constitutional, civil, criminal, commercial, tax, securities, monetary, competition, data-protection and IP law
instructions: Cite Republic Acts and codes by number and section; the Official Gazette (officialgazette.gov.ph) is the publication of record and LawPhil (lawphil.net) is the comprehensive free database of statutes, codes and jurisprudence; the Philippines is a mixed civil/common-law jurisdiction
Cite case law by SCRA/G.R. number: Supreme Court decisions (sc.judiciary.gov.ph, e-Library) as G.R. No. ..., date; the Supreme Court exercises constitutional review
For tax, the Bureau of Internal Revenue (bir.gov.ph) issues Revenue Regulations, Revenue Memorandum Circulars and BIR Rulings — administrative interpretation binding the Bureau

Route regulatory questions: securities and companies → SEC (sec.gov.ph); monetary policy and banking → Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (bsp.gov.ph); competition → Philippine Competition Commission (phcc.gov.ph); data protection → National Privacy Commission (privacy.gov.ph); intellectual property → IPOPHL (ipophil.gov.ph)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Republic Act/code (Official Gazette/LawPhil), (ii) decision (G.R. number), (iii) BIR issuance, (iv) regulator rule — and give the pinpoint (section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: lawphil.net is the practical free entry for statutes and Supreme Court jurisprudence; officialgazette.gov.ph is the publication of record
Many Philippine gov sites (officialgazette.gov.ph, sc.judiciary.gov.ph, congress.gov.ph, senate.gov.ph, bir.gov.ph, bsp.gov.ph) block bare curl but load via browser
BIR Revenue Regulations and Rulings are on bir.gov.ph; the Supreme Court e-Library is at elibrary.judiciary.gov.ph

name: Puerto Rico
description: Puerto Rico, principal legal disciplines: Commonwealth legislation and courts and the tax administration — overlaid by U.S. federal law, which applies in the territory
instructions: Puerto Rico is a U.S. territory with a civil-law-rooted mixed system: cite Commonwealth statutes from the Leyes de Puerto Rico via the Oficina de Servicios Legislativos (oslpr.org) and the Laws of Puerto Rico Annotated; U.S. federal law also applies — for federal questions use the United States source

Route by court: the Tribunal Supremo de Puerto Rico sits atop the Rama Judicial (poderjudicial.pr), cited by year TSPR number; federal matters go to the U.S. District Court for the District of Puerto Rico and the First Circuit
For Commonwealth tax, the Departamento de Hacienda (hacienda.pr.gov) administers the Código de Rentas Internas de Puerto Rico and issues cartas circulares and determinaciones administrativas
Administrative regulations (reglamentos) are filed with the Departamento de Estado / OGP (ogp.pr.gov)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) ley (Leyes de PR) or federal statute, (ii) opinión (Tribunal Supremo/federal), (iii) carta circular (Hacienda), (iv) reglamento — and give the pinpoint (artículo/section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: oslpr.org (Oficina de Servicios Legislativos) hosts the Leyes de Puerto Rico; ogp.pr.gov holds administrative regulations
Tribunal Supremo opinions are on poderjudicial.pr; remember U.S. federal law and the First Circuit also govern
Hacienda cartas circulares and determinaciones are on hacienda.pr.gov

name: Qatar
description: Qatar, all legal disciplines: the Al Meezan legal portal, the judiciary and the common-law Qatar Financial Centre, and the authorities across tax, monetary, financial-markets and commercial law
instructions: Cite legislation by law/decree number and article from Al Meezan, the Qatar Legal Portal (almeezan.qa), the official consolidated database (Arabic/English); Qatar is a civil-law/Sharia mixed system

Distinguish the onshore State system from the Qatar Financial Centre (qfc.qa), a common-law jurisdiction with its own civil and commercial regulations, regulator (QFCRA, qfcra.com) and the QICDRC courts; the onshore judiciary sits under the Supreme Judiciary Council (sjc.gov.qa)

For tax, the General Tax Authority (gta.gov.qa) administers corporate income tax and issues guidance; most Qatari-owned activity is outside income tax, with tax focused on foreign participation and the QFC regime

Route regulatory questions: monetary matters and banking → Qatar Central Bank (qcb.gov.qa); onshore securities markets → Qatar Financial Markets Authority (qfma.org.qa); QFC financial services → QFCRA (qfcra.com)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) law/decree (Al Meezan) or QFC regulation, (ii) court judgment (onshore or QICDRC), (iii) GTA guidance, (iv) regulator rule — and give the pinpoint (article) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: almeezan.qa is the official consolidated legal portal (Arabic/English) and the practical entry point for legislation
The QFC (qfc.qa, qfcra.com) is a separate common-law jurisdiction — treat its regulations and QICDRC judgments as distinct from onshore Qatari law
sjc.gov.qa and qfma.org.qa block bare curl but load via browser; GTA guidance is on gta.gov.qa

name: Romania
description: Romania, all legal disciplines: the consolidated legislation portal (legislatie.just.ro) and Monitorul Oficial, the Constitutional Court and the High Court of Cassation and Justice, both chambers of parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text on legislatie.just.ro (the Ministry of Justice portal): cite statutes by type/number/year (Legea nr. 287/2009 — Codul civil, art. 1357; Legea nr. 227/2015 — Codul fiscal) in the version-in-force; the promulgated text is in Monitorul Oficial (monitoruloficial.ro)

Route by court: constitutionality → Curtea Constituțională (ccr.ro, deciziile cited nr./year); civil, criminal and administrative cassation and appeals in the interest of the law → Înalta Curte de Casație și Justiție (iccj.ro), cited by section, decision number and year

For tax, distinguish binding law from ANAF's practice: the tax authority's guidance and the norme metodologice implementing the Codul fiscal (anaf.ro) bind the administration; anticipated individual tax solutions (soluția fiscală individuală anticipată) protect the requester

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: non-bank financial markets (capital, insurance, private pensions) → ASF (asfromania.ro); banking and monetary matters → Banca Națională a României (bnr.ro); competition → Consiliul Concurenței (consiliulconcurentei.ro); data protection → ANSPDCP (dataprotection.ro); industrial property → OSIM (osim.ro); medicines → ANMDMR (anm.ro)

Distinguish binding law from interpretive positions: BNR/ASF regulamente bind supervised entities while their guidance does not; ANAF ordine and circulare are administrative — label each and cite by number

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) lege/OUG/HG (legislatie.just.ro / Monitorul Oficial), (ii) decizie (CCR/ICCJ), (iii) normă metodologică/circulară, (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (articol, considerent) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: legislatie.just.ro provides consolidated 'forma actualizată' texts with document IDs; it blocks the sandbox's automated fetch but loads in a browser — open the specific act or use browser automation
The High Court migrated from scj.ro to iccj.ro (2021) — use iccj.ro; ICCJ and CCR publish decisions on their own sites by number/year
Monitorul Oficial (monitoruloficial.ro) is the authentic publication; free access to Part I is via the e-Monitor / Monitorul Oficial site
ANAF norme and guidance are under anaf.ro/legislatie; several Romanian sites return 503/timeouts intermittently — retry or use the browser

name: Slovakia
description: Slovakia, all legal disciplines: the official consolidated legal information system and gazette (Slov-Lex), the Constitutional, Supreme and Supreme Administrative Courts, parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text on Slov-Lex (slov-lex.sk), the official legal information system and electronic Zbierka zákonov: cite statutes by number/year and section (zákon č. 40/1964 Zb. Občiansky zákonník, § 420; zákon č. 595/2003 Z. z. o dani z príjmov)

Route by court: constitutionality → Ústavný súd (ustavnysud.sk, sp. zn. e.g. PL. ÚS / I. ÚS); civil and criminal → Najvyšší súd (nsud.sk); administrative and tax → Najvyšší správny súd (nssud.sk, the Supreme Administrative Court established 2021)
For tax, distinguish binding law from the Finančná správa practice: metodické pokyny and guidance (financnasprava.sk; Ministry of Finance on mfsr.sk) bind the administration, not the courts

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: banking, insurance, capital markets and monetary matters (integrated supervisor) → Národná banka Slovenska (nbs.sk); competition → Protimonopolný úrad (antimon.gov.sk); data protection → Úrad na ochranu osobných údajov (dataprotection.gov.sk); industrial property → ÚPV (indprop.gov.sk); medicines → ŠÚKL (sukl.sk)
Distinguish binding law from soft law: NBS opatrenia bind while its metodické usmernenia are guidance; tax metodické pokyny are administrative — label each and cite by number

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) zákon/vyhláška (Slov-Lex), (ii) rozhodnutie (sp. zn.), (iii) metodický pokyn, (iv) regulator guidance — and give the pinpoint (§, právna veta) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: slov-lex.sk is the official portal for consolidated legislation and the electronic Zbierka zákonov — search by number/year; it is the authoritative source

The Supreme Administrative Court (nssud.sk, since 2021) took over administrative and disciplinary case law from the Supreme Court; several court and parliament sites (nssud.sk, nrsr.sk, antimon.gov.sk) block bare curl but load via browser
Constitutional Court decisions are searchable at ustavnysud.sk by spisová značka
Finančná správa metodické pokyny are on financnasprava.sk under the methodical guidance section

name: Slovenia
description: Slovenia, all legal disciplines: the legal information system (PISRS) and the Uradni list gazette, the Constitutional and Supreme Courts and the case-law portal, parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text on PISRS (pisrs.si), the legal information system: cite statutes by short title and article (Obligacijski zakonik, OZ, 131. člen; Zakon o dohodnini, ZDoh-2) in the version-in-force; the promulgated text is in Uradni list (uradni-list.si)

Route by court: constitutionality → Ustavno sodišče (us-rs.si, cited e.g. U-I-.../ Up-...); ordinary and administrative case law is aggregated on the judiciary portal (sodisce.si, sodnapraksa.si) including the Vrhovno sodišče (Supreme Court)

For tax, distinguish binding law from the tax authority's practice: FURS guidance and pojasnila (fu.gov.si) state the administration's position; binding information (zavezujoča informacija) protects the requester

Route regulatory questions to the competent authority: banking and monetary matters → Banka Slovenije (bsi.si); securities markets → ATVP (a-tvp.si); competition → AVK (varstvo-konkurence.si); data protection → Informacijski pooblaščenec (ip-rs.si); industrial property → SIPO/URSIL (uil-sipo.si); medicines → JAZMP (jazmp.si)
Distinguish binding law from soft law: agency sklepi/regulations bind while pojasnila and smernice are guidance — label each and cite by number; identify EU implementation where relevant

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) zakon/uredba (PISRS/Uradni list), (ii) sodba (sodnapraksa.si), (iii) FURS pojasnilo, (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (člen, točka) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: pisrs.si serves consolidated legislation (NPB — neuradno prečiščeno besedilo) with stable IDs; uradni-list.si is the official gazette for the authentic text

sodnapraksa.si (reachable via sodisce.si) is the official case-law database for the Supreme, higher and administrative courts, searchable by opravilna številka; us-rs.si holds Constitutional Court decisions and blocks bare curl but loads via browser
The IP office site uil-sipo.si resolves on its www host; the office (URSIL) also has pages under gov.si — reach the register via its own application
dz-rs.si (National Assembly) blocks bare curl but loads via browser; it carries the legislative procedure

name: South Africa
description: South Africa, all legal disciplines: legislation (gov.za) and case law (SAFLII), the courts and Parliament, and the authorities across constitutional, civil, criminal, commercial, tax, monetary, financial, competition, data-protection and company/IP law
instructions: Cite statutes by short title, Act number and year and section from the legislation on gov.za (and SAFLII), e.g. Companies Act 71 of 2008 s.76; Income Tax Act 58 of 1962; South Africa is a mixed Roman-Dutch/common-law jurisdiction with a supreme Constitution

Cite case law by neutral citation: Constitutional Court as [2024] ZACC; Supreme Court of Appeal [2023] ZASCA; High Court [2023] ZAGPPHC — SAFLII (saflii.org) is the comprehensive free database, and judiciary.org.za carries court information
For tax, the South African Revenue Service (sars.gov.za) issues Interpretation Notes, Binding General/Private Rulings and practice notes — the binding rulings bind SARS on the stated facts

Route regulatory questions: monetary policy and prudential supervision → South African Reserve Bank (resbank.co.za, with the Prudential Authority); market conduct and financial services → FSCA (fsca.co.za); competition → the Competition Commission (compcom.co.za); data protection (POPIA) → the Information Regulator (inforegulator.org.za); companies and IP → CIPC (cipc.co.za)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Act/regulation (gov.za), (ii) judgment (neutral citation), (iii) SARS ruling/interpretation note, (iv) regulator rule — and give the pinpoint (section, paragraph) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: SAFLII (saflii.org) is the practical free entry for legislation and case law across all South African courts and blocks bare curl but loads via browser; gov.za hosts official Acts and gazettes
Several sites (gov.za, judiciary.org.za, parliament.gov.za, resbank.co.za, fsca.co.za, cipc.co.za) block bare curl but load via browser
SARS Interpretation Notes and Binding Rulings are on sars.gov.za and index by number
The twin-peaks model splits prudential (SARB/Prudential Authority) from conduct (FSCA)

name: Sri Lanka
description: Sri Lanka, principal legal disciplines: consolidated law and case law (LawNet), the Supreme Court and Parliament, and the authorities for tax, securities, monetary policy and IP
instructions: Cite statutes by Act name/number and section; LawNet (lawnet.gov.lk) holds consolidated legislation and case law and the Government Publications Bureau (documents.gov.lk) issues the Gazette and Acts; Sri Lanka is a mixed common-law/Roman-Dutch jurisdiction
Cite case law by reporter (SLR) or court: the Supreme Court (supremecourt.lk) is the apex court and exercises constitutional jurisdiction; Parliament (parliament.lk) legislates
For tax, the Inland Revenue Department (ird.gov.lk) administers the Inland Revenue Act and VAT and issues guidance binding the administration
Route regulatory questions: securities → SEC (sec.gov.lk); monetary policy and banking → Central Bank of Sri Lanka (cbsl.gov.lk); intellectual property → NIPO (nipo.gov.lk)
Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Act (LawNet/Gazette), (ii) judgment (SLR), (iii) IRD guidance, (iv) regulator rule — and give the pinpoint (section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: lawnet.gov.lk is the consolidated legislation and case-law portal; documents.gov.lk serves the Gazette and printed Acts
Several sites (lawnet.gov.lk, sec.gov.lk, cbsl.gov.lk) block bare curl or require the browser; ird.gov.lk may prompt authentication on some paths
Central Bank of Sri Lanka directions are on cbsl.gov.lk

name: Taiwan
description: Taiwan (ROC), all legal disciplines: the Laws & Regulations Database, the Judicial Yuan and the Legislative Yuan, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, monetary, competition, IP and drug law
instructions: Cite statutes by name and article from the Laws & Regulations Database of the Republic of China (law.moj.gov.tw), e.g. Civil Code art. 184; Income Tax Act; it is the official consolidated source with English translations

Route by court: the Judicial Yuan (judicial.gov.tw) houses the Supreme Court, the Supreme Administrative Court and the Constitutional Court (whose Justices issue Constitutional Court judgments, formerly Interpretations); the Legislative Yuan (ly.gov.tw) legislates
For tax, the Taxation Administration (dot.gov.tw) under the Ministry of Finance (mof.gov.tw) issues interpretive rulings (han-shih) binding the administration

Route regulatory questions: banking, securities, insurance (integrated regulator) → Financial Supervisory Commission (fsc.gov.tw); monetary policy → Central Bank (cbc.gov.tw); competition → Fair Trade Commission (ftc.gov.tw); intellectual property → TIPO (tipo.gov.tw); drugs and food → TFDA (fda.gov.tw)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) law/regulation (law.moj.gov.tw), (ii) judgment/Constitutional Court judgment (Judicial Yuan), (iii) MOF interpretive ruling, (iv) regulator rule — and give the pinpoint (article) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: law.moj.gov.tw is the authoritative consolidated legislation with English versions; nearly all other ROC gov sites (judicial.gov.tw, ly.gov.tw, mof.gov.tw, fsc.gov.tw, cbc.gov.tw, ftc.gov.tw, fda.gov.tw) block the sandbox's automated fetch but load via browser
Constitutional Court judgments (since the 2022 reform) and older J.Y. Interpretations are on the Judicial Yuan site (cons.judicial.gov.tw)
MOF interpretive tax rulings are searchable via dot.gov.tw and the MOF

name: Thailand
description: Thailand, all legal disciplines: the Royal Gazette and consolidated laws (Council of State), the Supreme and Constitutional Courts, Parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, securities, monetary, competition, data-protection and IP law
instructions: Cite legislation by act name and section; the Royal Gazette (ratchakitcha.soc.go.th) is the publication of record and the Office of the Council of State (krisdika.go.th) provides consolidated statutes (Civil and Commercial Code; Revenue Code)

Route by court: constitutionality → Constitutional Court (constitutionalcourt.or.th); civil and criminal → Supreme Court / Dika (supremecourt.or.th); administrative matters go to the Administrative Court; Parliament (parliament.go.th) legislates
For tax, the Revenue Department (rd.go.th) issues Departmental Instructions and rulings interpreting the Revenue Code, binding the administration

Route regulatory questions: securities and markets → SEC (sec.or.th); monetary policy and banking → Bank of Thailand (bot.or.th); competition → Trade Competition Commission (tcct.or.th); data protection (PDPA) → PDPC (pdpc.or.th); intellectual property → Department of Intellectual Property (ipthailand.go.th)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) act/royal decree (Royal Gazette/Council of State), (ii) decision (Supreme/Constitutional/Administrative Court), (iii) Revenue Department instruction, (iv) regulator rule — and give the pinpoint (section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: krisdika.go.th (Council of State) holds consolidated statutes with English translations of many codes; ratchakitcha.soc.go.th is the Royal Gazette
Most Thai gov sites (krisdika.go.th, supremecourt.or.th, parliament.go.th, bot.or.th, sec.or.th, ipthailand.go.th) block bare curl but load via browser
The Trade Competition Commission of Thailand is at tcct.or.th; Revenue Department instructions are on rd.go.th

name: Trinidad and Tobago
description: Trinidad and Tobago, all legal disciplines: the consolidated Laws, the Judiciary, Parliament, and the authorities for tax, monetary policy, banking and securities regulation
instructions: Cite legislation by chapter number and year from the Laws of Trinidad and Tobago on the Registrar General / Legal Affairs site (rgd.legalaffairs.gov.tt); Trinidad and Tobago is a common-law jurisdiction

Route by court: the Judiciary (ttlawcourts.org) comprises the High Court and Court of Appeal, with final appeal to the Privy Council (Trinidad and Tobago has not acceded to the CCJ's appellate jurisdiction) — cite by neutral citation; Parliament is at ttparliament.org
For tax, the Inland Revenue Division (ird.gov.tt) administers income, corporation and VAT law and issues guidance

Route regulatory questions: monetary matters and banking/insurance supervision → Central Bank of Trinidad and Tobago (central-bank.org.tt); securities → Trinidad and Tobago Securities and Exchange Commission (ttsec.org.tt)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) Act (Laws of T&T), (ii) judgment (High Court/CA/Privy Council), (iii) IRD guidance, (iv) regulator norm — and give the pinpoint (section) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: rgd.legalaffairs.gov.tt hosts the consolidated Laws of Trinidad and Tobago (it returned a transient 503 at verification; retry or use the browser)
ttlawcourts.org publishes Judiciary judgments; the TTSEC (ttsec.org.tt) and Central Bank (central-bank.org.tt) publish their guidelines
ird.gov.tt carries tax guidance and forms

name: Turkey
description: Turkey, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation (Mevzuat) and the Official Gazette, the Constitutional Court, the Court of Cassation and the Council of State, parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text on mevzuat.gov.tr: cite laws by number and article (6098 sayılı Türk Borçlar Kanunu, m. 49; 5520 sayılı Kurumlar Vergisi Kanunu) in the version-in-force; the official gazette is Resmî Gazete (resmigazete.gov.tr)

Route by court: constitutionality and individual applications → Anayasa Mahkemesi (anayasa.gov.tr); civil and criminal cassation → Yargıtay (yargitay.gov.tr); administrative and tax → Danıştay / Council of State (danistay.gov.tr)

For tax, the Gelir İdaresi Başkanlığı (gib.gov.tr) issues tebliğ (communiqués), sirküler and özelge (private rulings); tebliğ published in the Resmî Gazete are binding general regulation, özelge protect the requester

Route regulatory questions: capital markets → SPK (spk.gov.tr); banking supervision → BDDK (bddk.org.tr); monetary matters → TCMB (tcmb.gov.tr); competition → Rekabet Kurumu (rekabet.gov.tr); data protection → KVKK (kvkk.gov.tr); industrial property → TÜRKPATENT (turkpatent.gov.tr); medicines → TİTCK (titck.gov.tr)

Distinguish binding law from interpretive positions: tebliğ bind, sirküler and özelge are administrative; SPK/BDDK tebliğ bind supervised entities — label each and cite by number and Resmî Gazete date

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) kanun/yönetmelik (Mevzuat/Resmî Gazete), (ii) court decision, (iii) tebliğ/özelge, (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (madde) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: mevzuat.gov.tr provides consolidated statutes, regulations and communiqués; resmigazete.gov.tr is the authentic gazette (search by date)
Constitutional Court decisions (incl. bireysel başvuru) are on anayasa.gov.tr and Kararlar Bilgi Bankası; yargitay.gov.tr and bddk.org.tr block bare curl but load via browser
GİB tax communiqués, circulars and rulings are on gib.gov.tr; özelge are in the özelge sistemi
SPK and BDDK publish their tebliğ and decisions on their own sites

name: Ukraine
description: Ukraine, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation (Verkhovna Rada database), the Constitutional and Supreme Courts and the unified register of court decisions, parliament, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial, competition, data protection, IP and health law
instructions: The primary norm is the consolidated text in the Verkhovna Rada legislation database (zakon.rada.gov.ua): cite statutes by name and article (Цивільний кодекс, ст. 1166; Податковий кодекс) in the version-in-force

Route by court: constitutionality → Constitutional Court (ccu.gov.ua); civil, criminal, commercial and administrative cassation → Supreme Court (supreme.court.gov.ua); all court decisions are in the Unified State Register of Court Decisions (reyestr.court.gov.ua), searchable by case number
For tax, the State Tax Service (tax.gov.ua) issues individual tax consultations (ІПК) and generalised consultations; ІПК protect the taxpayer who relies on them

Route regulatory questions: banking, monetary matters and (since 2020) most non-bank financial supervision → National Bank of Ukraine (bank.gov.ua); securities and stock market → NSSMC (nssmc.gov.ua); competition → AMCU (amcu.gov.ua); data protection → the Parliament Commissioner for Human Rights / Ombudsman (ombudsman.gov.ua); IP → the National IP Office UKRNOIVI (nipo.gov.ua); medicines → the State Service on Medicines (dls.gov.ua)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) law/act (zakon.rada.gov.ua), (ii) court decision (reyestr), (iii) tax consultation (ІПК), (iv) regulator soft law — and give the pinpoint (стаття, частина) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: zakon.rada.gov.ua is the authoritative consolidated legislation database with stable act IDs and version history; rada.gov.ua carries the legislative process
reyestr.court.gov.ua is the Unified State Register of Court Decisions — the single fullest source of Ukrainian case law, searchable by case number
tax.gov.ua and amcu.gov.ua block bare curl but load via browser; the NBU (bank.gov.ua) absorbed non-bank financial supervision under the 2020 'split-up' reform
Some Ukrainian sites have intermittent availability; retry or use the browser

name: United Nations
description: United Nations legal materials: the UN Charter and system, the Treaty Collection, official documents and digital library, UNCITRAL, the Office of Legal Affairs, the OHCHR human-rights system and the International Court of Justice
instructions: Cite UN treaties from the UN Treaty Collection (treaties.un.org) by title, date and UNTS volume/registration number, noting status (signature, ratification, entry into force) from the depositary status pages

Cite UN documents by their symbol (e.g. A/RES/76/..., S/RES/..., A/CN.9/...) — undocs.org resolves a symbol directly to the document and the Digital Library (digitallibrary.un.org) holds voting and drafting records

For international trade law, cite UNCITRAL instruments and travaux from uncitral.un.org (Model Laws, the CISG, arbitration rules); the Office of Legal Affairs (legal.un.org) hosts the International Law Commission materials and the Audiovisual Library

For human rights, cite treaty-body general comments, concluding observations and individual communications from OHCHR (ohchr.org); for inter-State disputes and advisory opinions cite the International Court of Justice (icj-cij.org) by case name and year

Distinguish binding instruments (ratified treaties, Security Council decisions under Chapter VII) from recommendatory ones (most General Assembly resolutions, model laws, general comments) — label each and cite by its symbol or registration

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) treaty (UNTS), (ii) organ resolution/decision (document symbol), (iii) ICJ judgment/advisory opinion, (iv) treaty-body or ILC output — and give the pinpoint (article, paragraph) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: undocs.org is the fastest path from a document symbol to full text in all six official languages; treaties.un.org gives authoritative depositary status and UNTS texts
UNCITRAL Model Laws and the CISG, with status tables and travaux, are on uncitral.un.org; the ILC materials and the UN Audiovisual Library of International Law are under legal.un.org
OHCHR (ohchr.org) hosts the treaty-body database (general comments, concluding observations, jurisprudence); ICJ judgments and pleadings are on icj-cij.org
digitallibrary.un.org adds voting records and drafting history useful for interpretation

name: Uruguay
description: Uruguay, all legal disciplines: consolidated legislation and the Diario Oficial (IMPO), Parliament, the Judiciary, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, financial and data-protection law
instructions: Cite legislation by law number and article from IMPO (impo.com.uy), which holds consolidated texts and the Diario Oficial (Código Civil; Texto Ordenado de la DGI); IMPO is the official publication and consolidation body
The Parliament (parlamento.gub.uy) enacts law and its site carries the legislative record; the Poder Judicial (poderjudicial.gub.uy) is the judiciary, with the Suprema Corte de Justicia at the apex
For tax, the Dirección General Impositiva (dgi.gub.uy) issues consultas and resoluciones binding the administration; its Texto Ordenado consolidates the tax laws

Route regulatory questions: banking, securities, insurance and pensions supervision plus monetary matters (integrated in the central bank) → Banco Central del Uruguay (bcu.gub.uy); data protection (URCDP), competition and other agencies sit under the unified gub.uy portal, routed by path; presidencia.gub.uy carries decretos

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) ley/decreto (IMPO/Diario Oficial), (ii) sentencia (Poder Judicial), (iii) consulta/resolución DGI, (iv) regulator norm — and give the pinpoint (artículo) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: impo.com.uy is the authoritative consolidated legislation and gazette; search by law number or the Texto Ordenado
The BCU (bcu.gub.uy) integrates all financial supervision through its Superintendencia de Servicios Financieros; it blocks bare curl but loads via browser
URCDP (data protection) and the competition authority are under gub.uy/<unidad>
DGI Texto Ordenado and consultas are on dgi.gub.uy

name: Vietnam
description: Vietnam, all legal disciplines: the National Database of Legal Documents and the government portal, the Supreme People's Court and the National Assembly, and the authorities across civil, criminal, commercial, tax, securities, monetary, competition, IP and drug law
instructions: Cite legislation by type/number and article from the National Database of Legal Documents (vbpl.vn), e.g. Civil Code (Bo luat Dan su) 2015; Law on Enterprises; the government portal (chinhphu.vn) carries decrees (nghi dinh) and the official gazette

Distinguish the hierarchy: laws (luat) of the National Assembly (quochoi.vn) → decrees (nghi dinh) of the Government → circulars (thong tu) of ministries; the Supreme People's Court (toaan.gov.vn) issues resolutions and selects precedents (an le) with binding effect
For tax, the General Department of Taxation (gdt.gov.vn) issues official letters (cong van) and guidance interpreting the tax laws, binding the administration

Route regulatory questions: securities → State Securities Commission (ssc.gov.vn); monetary policy and banking → State Bank of Vietnam (sbv.gov.vn); competition → the Vietnam Competition Commission (vcca.gov.vn); intellectual property → IP Vietnam (ipvietnam.gov.vn); drugs → Drug Administration of Vietnam (dav.gov.vn)

Always classify what supports a claim — (i) luat/nghi dinh/thong tu (vbpl.vn/chinhphu.vn), (ii) precedent/resolution (SPC), (iii) GDT official letter, (iv) regulator rule — and give the pinpoint (dieu/article) where the wording is decisive
search_notes: vbpl.vn is the National Database of Legal Documents; most Vietnamese gov sites (vbpl.vn, toaan.gov.vn, gdt.gov.vn, vcca.gov.vn, dav.gov.vn) block bare curl but load via browser
Court precedents (an le) and resolutions are published by the Supreme People's Court (toaan.gov.vn; congbobanan.toaan.gov.vn for judgments)
GDT interpretive official letters (cong van) are the practical tax doctrine on gdt.gov.vn
